[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · autumn 2026-27 (Component 1 Portfolio, G11)

Grading follows the [[shared/bnds-assessment-framework|BNDS assessment framework]]: **Attainment 50% · Course Skills 20% · Learning Behaviour 10% · End of Term Exam 20%**. A1–A4 are the attainment layer (portfolio milestones marked against the 9479 AOs); CS1–CS10 are the course-skills layer, each direct practice for the attainment it precedes; the 20% End of Term category is met by **the Final** (below).

The dates below may shift when the school publishes its calendar for this period.

## Attainments (50%) — A1–A4

| Code | Date | Milestone | AOs | Unit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A1 | **Fri 2026-10-09** | Recording: intention, Stage 1 pages, sustained study, supporting studies | AO1 | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study\|Unit 2]] |
| A2 | **Mon 2026-11-09** | Exploration & artist study: twelve experiments, media selection, scale trial, artist pages, bridge piece | AO2+AO3 | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study\|Unit 3]] |

## The Final (End of Term Exam category, 20%)

The 20% category is satisfied by **the Final**: a teacher-marked assessment of your portfolio-so-far against the Cambridge criteria. There is no exam paper, no invigilation and no new task — you are marked on the portfolio you produce over the semester. The date will be confirmed in class.

classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-d.body:
# A Level Art & Design

> **Start here:** [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-course-map|Course Map]] (all four semesters)
> **Semester plans:** [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|S1]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|S2]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-3-plan|S3]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|S4]]
> **Assessment registers:** [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|S1]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s2-assessments|S2]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s3-assessments|S3]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s4-assessments|S4]]

**Cambridge International AS & A Level Art & Design (9479)** at BNDS, on the **revised 2028–2030 syllabus** (first examination 2028). This is the G11 course beginning autumn 2026 (Year 1 = 2026–27, Year 2 = 2027–28). It follows on from [[classes/pre-a-level-art-design/pre-a-level-art-design|Pre A Level Art & Design]] (G10) and is graded per [[shared/bnds-assessment-framework|the BNDS framework]].

**Entry: linear A Level, June 2028.** All three components are entered in one series, so there is no separate AS award and no carry-forward step, and spring 2027 carries no Cambridge deadline. You submit at a single point, around **end April 2028**, where C1, C2 and C3 are handed in together ([[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-course-map|course map]], S4).

## Syllabus at a glance (2028–2030)

| | C1: Portfolio | C2: Externally Set Assignment | C3: Personal Investigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Marks | 60 → weighted 90 | 60 | 75 → weighted 150 |
| Weight | 60% AS / 30% AL | 40% AS / 20% AL | **50% AL** |
| Nature | Internally set, externally marked; selection of work over the course | Question paper, **three themes** to choose from; supporting studies + final outcome in a **15-hour supervised test** | Theme-based investigation: **2000–2500-word written discourse** integrated with a body of visual work + bibliography |

**AOs:** AO1 Record, AO2 Explore, AO3 Develop, AO4 Present. Marks per AO — C1: 15/15/20/10; C2: 15/15/15/15; C3: 20/20/25/10. Qualification weightings: AS 25/25/29/21, AL 25/25/31/19.

Areas of study: Fine art / Graphic communication / 3D design / Textiles-Fashion (these may differ per component; the same work may never go to two components). All submission is digital (no hyperlinks/QR). C1 can be carried forward alone; C3 cannot. Private candidates cannot enter. Annotation is assessed within AO3 and must be typed. Generative-AI misuse counts as plagiarism.

## Course shape (the four-semester pipeline)

Every semester runs the same **eight-stage portfolio pipeline** (foundations → recording/sustained study → exploration/artist study → development → final outcome/portfolio), with 4 Attainments as unit gates.

| Semester | Component | Plan |
|---|---|---|
| S1 autumn 2026 | **C1 Portfolio** | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan\|Semester 1 Plan]] |
| S2 spring 2027 | C3 part 1 (theme → mid-point portfolio) | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan\|Semester 2 Plan]] |
| S3 autumn 2027 | C3 part 2 (finish, internal submission) | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-3-plan\|Semester 3 Plan]] |
| S4 spring 2028 | C2 (15-hour test in Qingming mock week) + all-component submission | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan\|Semester 4 Plan]] |

Dates for S2 onward are provisional and may shift once the school publishes the calendar for those periods.

Detail across all four: [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-course-map|Course Map]].

classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/.body:
# 9479 S1 Lesson 01: Introduction and the Theme Menu

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-1-foundations-on-the-theme|Unit 1]] · Lesson 01 of 15 · [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-02-formal-elements-on-the-theme|L02]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 4 days (W1) |
| Dates | Tue 2026-09-01 → Fri 2026-09-04 |
| AO focus | AO1 Record, orientation |
| Deliverable | Committed theme with signed commitment card; source folder with first photographs; first line studies |
| Graded | Nothing is graded in W1–W2. The day-3 diagnostic is not marked but sets your baseline. |
| Homework | Reflection, 150 words: why your chosen theme can sustain four months (due Monday) |

## Goal

By Friday you will know how the portfolio is marked, will have read what a top-band portfolio actually does, will have committed to one theme from the menu, and will have begun looking at it. The theme decision is the most consequential of the semester, and you make it against the criteria, not by mood.

## How it runs

- **Tue 09-01 — opener, no content.** Introductions, getting to know the group, why this subject, how the course runs. Closes with the semester map (five units, four attainments, no spare days) and the four AOs in plain words — orientation only, no portfolio work and nothing graded. This is a linear June 2028 entry: a two-year course.
- **Wed 09-02 — portfolio day.** One finished 96/100 portfolio shown end to end, top band vs middle band two pages side by side, then portfolio reading in pairs against the AO-language worksheet. The theme menu is presented with its criteria — no choosing yet. Exit with a shortlist of two themes and one line of reasoning for each.
- **Thu 09-03** — diagnostic drawing (one observed object, pencil only; kept, not marked); **theme commitment** signed off one-to-one against the five criteria; commitment card: theme, three source locations, first shoot date.
- **Fri 09-04** — source folder setup (one phone folder, one physical); line-study demo (continuous then structural); line studies from first-hand objects within the theme.

## Key content: the theme menu

A theme earns its place only if it passes all five criteria: (1) primary sources photographable within daily reach all semester; (2) survives four months of looking; (3) genuinely carries line, tone, texture and color; (4) opens both 2D and 3D development; (5) has a deep artist reference pool to draw from.

- **Reflections and Surfaces** — glass, water, screens, mirrors, polished metal in daily reach; distortion gives instant composition.
- **Worn and Weathered** — rust, peeling paint, cracked walls, mended objects; the strongest texture theme, with an excellent 3D loop into casting and relief.
- **Hands at Work** — people making and doing; figure drawing with a reason. This needs willing subjects, so check your access before you commit.
- **Structures of the Everyday** — the stacked, shelved and scaffolded; strong for line and shape. The risk is coldness, so your annotation must carry the why.

classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/.body:
# 9479 S1 Lesson 02: Formal Elements on the Theme

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-1-foundations-on-the-theme|Unit 1]] · Lesson 02 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-01-introduction-and-the-theme-menu|L01]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-03-intention-and-composition-planning|L03]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 6 days (W2–W3) |
| Dates | Mon 2026-09-07 → Fri 2026-09-11, plus Mon 2026-09-14 |
| AO focus | AO1 Record |
| Deliverable | Formal elements study set (line, tone and form, texture, color) and Recording Page 1; digital portfolio set up |
| Feeds | CS1 collected Mon 2026-09-14 — practice for A1 |
| Homework | Sketchbook: one further study per night from the theme, alternating element; ten minutes each, dated |

## Goal

You work each formal element directly on your own theme. By Monday you will have looked at the theme through five different instruments, and your first portfolio page can argue something. This lesson ends Unit 1.

## How it runs

- **Mon 09-07** — contour line vs structural line: two A4 line studies of the same object, different instrument; annotate which line told the truth.
- **Tue 09-08** — nine-step tonal ladder demo, then one tonal study from a theme source, charcoal. No outlines.
- **Wed 09-09** — push the tonal study: larger, darker range, further than feels safe. Failure is the point; annotate the failure and its lesson.
- **Thu 09-10** — mark inventory (six tools); surface matching: three textures from theme sources, each rendered with the mark that fits it; peers match mark to photographed surface.
- **Fri 09-11** — limited-palette mixing toward an observed color; three color studies with swatch, mixing route, color in context.
- **Mon 09-14** — the exemplar's opening page read as an argument; compose Recording Page 1 from the week's studies, selection first; set up your digital portfolio from the template. **CS1 collected.**

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Assessment

**CS1** (formal elements study set + Recording Page 1, sketchbook) collects Mon 2026-09-14 — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]]. Marked for evidence of observation and honest annotation, not polish; returned inside five working days.

# 9479 S1 Lesson 03: Intention and Composition Planning

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]] · Lesson 03 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-02-formal-elements-on-the-theme|L02]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-04-the-sustained-study|L04]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 4 days (W3) — Unit 2 opens |
| Dates | Tue 2026-09-15 → Fri 2026-09-18 |
| AO focus | AO1 Record |
| Deliverable | Written intention; mind map; thumbnail sheet with one composition chosen and the rejections annotated |
| Feeds | CS2 collected Fri 2026-09-18 — direct practice for A1 |
| Homework | Written annotation: expand the intention to a paragraph a stranger could follow |

## Goal

In this lesson the theme becomes an investigation. By Friday you can say, in writing, what you are looking at and why, you have opened the theme wider than your first idea, and you have chosen one composition to sustain — with the rejections written down. Choosing and rejecting in writing is the top-band habit this lesson builds.

## How it runs

- **Tue 09-15** — two exemplar intentions read aloud (one strong, one vague); drafting frame on the board: *I am investigating / because / I will record*; write, swap, tighten — your partner strikes every empty word.
- **Wed 09-16** — mind mapping from the object outward (material, function, memory, opposite); A3 mind map, at least three branches past the first idea; circle the branch that surprised you.
- **Thu 09-17** — the thumbnail as a cheap test (5×7 cm, tone blocked, thirty seconds of decision each); six or more thumbnails from the mind map's strongest branches; squint-test pin-up.
- **Fri 09-18** — re-read your intention against the surviving thumbnails; choose one; annotate every rejection with one honest line; assemble the CS2 sheet. **CS2 collected.** The sustained study begins Sunday.

## Assessment

**CS2** (intention statement + mind map, written annotation) is collected Fri 2026-09-18 — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]]. It is direct practice for A1: your intention and mind map reappear inside the recording milestone.

Dates for this period may shift when the school publishes its calendar.

# 9479 S1 Lesson 04: The Sustained Study

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]] · Lesson 04 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-03-intention-and-composition-planning|L03]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-05-recording-pages-and-a1|L05]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 5 days (W4, includes Sunday make-up day) |
| Dates | Sun 2026-09-20 → Thu 2026-09-24 |
| AO focus | AO1 Record |
| Deliverable | Stage 1 page composed; the sustained observed study carried across two days; supporting color and texture studies |
| Feeds | CS3 progress check Thu 2026-09-24 |
| Homework | Sketchbook: ten minutes of observed drawing per night from your chosen composition's source |

## Goal

One long observed study, started and finished without restarting, is the spine of AO1. You build the Stage 1 page, then carry the sustained study across a real two-day run, then support it with focused color and texture work. Stamina is the content.

## How it runs

- **Sun 09-20** (make-up day) — demo on Stage 1 page architecture: hierarchy, breathing room, annotation placement. Compose your Stage 1 page from Lesson 3's material; photograph and file it.
- **Mon 09-21** — spend five minutes with the source before any mark, then begin the sustained study: light structural drawing, commit to placement. The sheet you start today finishes Wednesday. Cover, do not erase.
- **Tue 09-22 → Wed 09-23** — two-day run, one sheet. Tuesday builds the tonal structure; Wednesday resolves edges, surface and depth. There is a midpoint checkpoint each day, and the conference rota runs.
- **Thu 09-24** — two focused A5 color and texture studies supporting the main study; **CS3** walked progress check against the AO1 descriptors (criteria read aloud, date on the board: Fri 2026-10-09); holiday sketchbook brief.

These dates may shift when the school publishes its calendar for this period.

## Assessment

**CS3** (sustained-study progress check — walked, not collected; recorded on the rota sheet) sits Thu 2026-09-24, in the same session — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

# 9479 S1 Lesson 06: Media Exploration

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] · Lesson 06 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-05-recording-pages-and-a1|L05]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-07-selection-and-scale|L07]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 5 days (W7) — Unit 3 opens |
| Dates | Mon 2026-10-12 → Fri 2026-10-16 |
| AO focus | AO2 Explore, with AO3 opening |
| Deliverable | Twelve media experiments run as one three-day session; a keep-or-change decision made with evidence |
| Feeds | CS5 collected Fri 2026-10-16 |
| Homework | Sketchbook: continue the two most promising experiments at home, ten minutes each |

## Goal

You run twelve prescribed experiments in three days to find which media carry the theme, not which describe it. Experiments are allowed to fail; safe tests teach nothing. Your decision at the end is made with evidence, and you name the rejections.

## Key content: the twelve experiments

Run in three blocks of four, one block per day, on A5 unless stated. Every experiment uses your own source material.

1. ink and stick, blind then sighted
2. charcoal laid then erased back
3. watercolor wet-in-wet, tone only
4. collage cut from your own printed photographs
5. monoprint, one pull
6. frottage from actual theme surfaces
7. wax resist under ink wash
8. acrylic impasto with card scraper
9. pen stippling or cross-hatching, one passage pushed to full dark
10. mixed-media layering, any three stacked
11. **low-relief test**: card or clay maquette photographed in raking light (the 2D-to-3D loop)
12. digital pass: your own photograph through threshold and overlay, printed

## How it runs

- **Mon 10-12** — the twelve are presented with one exemplar strip each; plan the exploration (which source feeds which experiment, formats and order fixed); stations set up.
- **Tue 10-13 → Thu 10-15** — three-day run: experiments 1–4, 5–8, 9–12, one block per day, roughly ten minutes each, annotating as you go; each day closes by circling the most surprising result.
- **Fri 10-16** — lay out all twelve in grid order; keep-or-change sort (carry forward / adapt / drop) with a written reason for every drop; **CS5 collected**; preview of testing the keepers at scale.

## Assessment

**CS5** (twelve experiments documented, sketchbook) collects Fri 2026-10-16 — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]]. At least one experiment must fail, and its annotation says so and says why.

The collection date may move to the following Monday if Foreign Culture Day falls on that Friday; this will be confirmed in class.

Everything you have recorded so far becomes pages an examiner could read, then is looked at together. The holiday splits this lesson: you build the pages before it, the review happens after it, and a self-check on return helps you see what AO1 actually asks.

The exploration becomes a commitment. The class critiques the experiment grids, you select your media for reasons you can write down, the selection is tested at real scale, and the scale trial's failures are fixed. The Stage 3 page then shows the exploration as reasoning, not as a swatch chart.

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-4-development|Unit 4]] · Lesson 10 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-09-artist-2-bridge-piece-and-a2|L09]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-11-composition-2-and-critique|L11]] →

## Goal

Development begins with making, not talking. Compose Composition 1 from your own recorded studies, never from memory, then build it across two uninterrupted days, resolve it, and document it properly. Unresolved work cannot be judged; badly photographed work loses real marks.

## How it runs

- **Wed 11-11** — composing from studies: tracing-paper overlays, the direction sentence taped to the board edge; compose Composition 1 as a full-size cartoon or worked drawing; sign-off rota begins.

# 9479 S1 Lesson 08: Artist Study 1

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] · Lesson 08 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-07-selection-and-scale|L07]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-09-artist-2-bridge-piece-and-a2|L09]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 5 days (W9) — first use of the artist study template |
| Dates | Mon 2026-10-26 → Fri 2026-10-30 |
| AO focus | AO3 Develop, contextual study |
| Deliverable | Artist pages: analysis, meaning and context, method study, personal response, influence note |
| Homework | Written annotation: draft the meaning-and-context paragraph at home |

These dates are provisional and may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Goal

Choose your first artist because they solve the problem in your work, not because you like them. The week runs the full artist study arc: analyze, understand in context, copy the method to learn it, then answer the artist with your own work. Influence must be visible in the work, not only claimed in the writing.

## Key content: prescribed artist pools, by theme

Choose from the pool for your committed theme. A swap outside the pool needs a one-to-one case.

- **Reflections and Surfaces** — Richard Estes (urban glass), Janet Fish (glass, light, still-life color), M. C. Escher (reflecting-sphere drawings).
- **Worn and Weathered** — Antoni Tàpies (matter and surface), Aaron Siskind (photographed peeling walls), Anselm Kiefer (material and memory, for stronger students).
- **Hands at Work** — Käthe Kollwitz (working hands and figures), Albrecht Dürer (praying-hands studies), Yoshitoshi or a contemporary craft documentary photographer as a non-European anchor.
- **Structures of the Everyday** — Charles Sheeler (industrial structure), Lyonel Feininger (architecture into geometry), Rachel Whiteread (structure into 3D casting — feeds the 3D loop).

## How it runs

- **Mon 10-26** — pools presented, two works per artist; choose and begin the analysis (composition, tone, mark, color); write your choice rationale: which problem this artist solves.
- **Tue 10-27** — meaning and context: what the work meant then, what it means for you now; writing frame for the ESL register; peer read strikes every sentence that could describe any artist.
- **Wed 10-28** — method study: copy a passage of the artist's method in the artist's logic; annotate what the copying taught you.
- **Thu 10-29** — personal response: your own source worked through what the artist taught; exit: name the artist's fingerprint in your piece.
- **Fri 10-30** — the influence note: point at the evidence; assemble the artist pages; **CS7 collected.**

## Assessment

**CS7** (artist analysis pages, written annotation) is collected Fri 2026-10-30 — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

## Goal

Composition 2 is the alternative you did not take in Lesson 3's thumbnails, built with the same effort as the first so the comparison is honest. You then critique both against your intention and change something because of what you heard.

## How it runs

- **Thu 11-19** — build composition 2: structure and main relationships in one day. The pace is deliberate.

- **Mon 11-23** — pin both compositions with your intention between them; structured critique, full protocol, whole class in rounds: describe, evaluate against intention, one suggestion; the maker speaks last, notes taken verbatim; each maker circles the two comments that matter.

**CS8** (both compositions photographed and laid out) collects Fri 2026-11-20 — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]]. [[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-4-development|Unit 4]] · Lesson 12 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-11-composition-2-and-critique|L11]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-13-final-plan-and-a3|L13]] →

## Goal

You make the real piece in one continuous run: underdrawing through to surface across four days, a hard look on Friday while there is still time to change things, then three days to resolve the focal areas and unify the whole. The plan from Lesson 13 governs every session. If you want to change the plan, you discuss it — it is a conference, not a drift.

## How it runs

- **Mon 12-14 → Thu 12-17** — four-day build run, one continuous session: day 1 underdrawing and block-in; day 2 mid-stages (main relationships established); day 3 production, with the conference rota concentrated here; day 4 detail and surface. Each day: re-entry look, build, exit annotation against the plan.
- **Fri 12-18** — mid-production review: the piece and the plan side by side, teacher and peer pass, protocol applied, changes listed and ranked. **CS10**: write and hand in the review reflection — what changes, in what order, and why.
- **Mon 12-21 → Wed 12-23** — three-day resolve run, opening with the criteria read and the date on the board (Thu 2027-01-07): day 6 focal areas; day 7 surface unity; day 8 final touches and stop — you declare the piece finished in writing.

# 9479 S1 Lesson 15: Submission Week and A4

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-5-final-outcome-and-portfolio|Unit 5]] · Lesson 15 of 15 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-14-the-final-outcome|L14]]

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Sessions | 5 days, 7 sessions (W19) — Unit 5 and the semester close |
| Dates | Mon 2027-01-04 → Fri 2027-01-08 |
| AO focus | AO4 Present, across AO1–AO4 |
| Deliverable | Your portfolio photographed, sequenced, edited and submitted; A4 sat; the semester reflected on |
| Feeds | **A4 Thu 2027-01-07**: final portfolio, marked against AO1–AO4; no edits after. Marking window Thu 01-07 → Wed 01-20 |
| Homework | Reflection: the semester look-back, finished after Friday. Winter homework: three candidate C3 themes with annotated references |

## Goal

Submission is a supervised craft, not a scramble. Monday you photograph the outcome properly, because the photograph is the version the examiner sees. Tuesday you sequence the whole journey so it reads in order. Wednesday you cut whatever does not earn its place and check every AO. Thursday it goes in, and nothing is edited after. Friday you look back.

## How it runs

- **Mon 01-04** (two beats) — photograph the outcome: rig, light, shoot, detail passes; prepare the images: crop, correct, name, file to the template.
- **Tue 01-05** — sequencing logic: read the exemplar's page order as a story; sequence your portfolio, lay out the full run order and walk it once; peer walk: where does a stranger stumble.
- **Wed 01-06** (two beats) — edit and clean: cut what does not earn its place, log every cut; final AO check: all four descriptors, in writing, signed.
- **Thu 01-07** — **A4 attainment.** Final portfolio due and submitted in session. No edits after.
- **Fri 01-08** — submission acknowledged; what happens next (marking window, grades Wed 2027-01-20, return in W21); write your semester reflection; the C3 bridge: what a Personal Investigation is, and the winter homework — three candidate themes with annotated references.

## Assessment

You sit **A4** (final portfolio: the resolved outcome plus the complete supporting portfolio, sequenced and edited) on Thu 2027-01-07 — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]]. The sequence must read the whole journey in order without narration: theme, recording, exploration, artists, development, outcome.

## After the semester

W20 (Mon 01-11 →) is the exam window: no Art and Design classes. The End of Term Exam format for this studio course will be confirmed in class. W21 returns portfolios with one-to-one feedback Tue–Thu; grades and comments due Wed 2027-01-20. Winter vacation runs 2027-01-24 → 2027-02-21; your three C3 theme candidates open the Semester 2 consultation cycle on Mon 2027-02-22.

These dates may shift when the school publishes its calendar for that period.

classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/94.body:
# 9479 S1 Unit 1: Foundations on the Theme

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 1 of 5 · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]] →

## What this unit does

This unit opens the Component 1 portfolio work at its foundations stage: how the portfolio is marked (the four AOs), what a top-band portfolio does, and the semester's most important decision — committing to one theme from the menu against set criteria. You then work the formal elements directly on your committed theme (AO1 Record focus), so that by the end of the unit you have looked at the theme through five instruments and can build a first portfolio page that argues something.

## Dates & span

Tue 2026-09-01 → Mon 2026-09-14 (W1 → W3 Monday; 10 sessions). There are no attainment grades in W1–W2. On day 3 you do a diagnostic drawing, which sets your baseline.

## Lessons

- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-01-introduction-and-the-theme-menu|Lesson 01: Introduction and the Theme Menu]] — 96/100 exemplar briefing, the four AOs, diagnostic drawing, theme committed Thu against five criteria, first sources and line studies.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-02-formal-elements-on-the-theme|Lesson 02: Formal Elements on the Theme]] — line, tone-builds-form, push-to-fail, mark/surface matching, color from observation; Recording Page 1 ("a page that argues") and digital portfolio setup. Ends the unit.

## Assessment

No attainment grades in this unit. It contains one CS task: **CS1** (formal elements study set + Recording Page 1, sketchbook), collected Mon 2026-09-14 — practice toward A1. See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- **The theme menu:** Reflections and Surfaces · Worn and Weathered · Hands at Work · Structures of the Everyday. The final list will be confirmed in class. Your theme must pass all five criteria: photographable primary sources within daily reach; survives four months of looking; carries line, tone, texture and color; opens both 2D and 3D; a deep artist reference pool to draw from. Sign-off is one-to-one, and the commitment card is physical.
- **Source rule:** "Nothing found, nothing borrowed" — every source is your own photograph or observation.
- **Standing protocols:** studio session shape (entry look → demo → sustained work with midpoint checkpoint → exit annotation; conference rota 4–5 students/day), annotation rule (record decisions, rejections and observations, not feelings), homework rule (sketchbook / annotation / reflection only, ≤30 min).

# 9479 S1 Unit 2: Recording & Sustained Study

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 2 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-1-foundations-on-the-theme|Unit 1]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] →

## What this unit does

This is the recording / sustained-study stage of the course, working entirely under AO1 (Record). You turn the theme into a written investigation (intention, mind map, thumbnails with annotated rejections), then build one long observed study carried across two days without restarting. Everything you record becomes pages that can be read by an examiner. The unit closes with A1, the semester's first attainment.

## Dates & span

Tue 2026-09-15 → Sat 2026-10-10 (W3 → W6), split by the National Day break. This includes the make-up days on Sun 2026-09-20 and Sat 2026-10-10 (Sports Day afternoon).

## Lessons

- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-03-intention-and-composition-planning|Lesson 03: Intention and Composition Planning]] — written intention, mind map, ≥6 thumbnails, one composition chosen, rejections annotated.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-04-the-sustained-study|Lesson 04: The Sustained Study]] — Stage 1 page; the sustained study across a two-day run.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-05-recording-pages-and-a1|Lesson 05: Recording Pages and A1]] — your own photography, studies brought to one standard, recording pages sequenced; AO1 self-check; A1 sat and fed back.

## Assessment

**A1** is on **Fri 2026-10-09** — the recording milestone (intention, Stage 1 pages, sustained study, supports), marked against AO1. It feeds the mid-term grades due Thu 2026-10-29. Check tasks inside the unit, each direct practice for A1:

- **CS2** Fri 2026-09-18 (intention + mind map)
- **CS3** Thu 2026-09-24 (sustained-study walked check)
- **CS4** Wed 2026-09-30 (recording pages build check)

See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- Your sustained-study media are assigned to you individually, based on the W1 diagnostic.
- Use the intention drafting frame (*I am investigating / because / I will record*) to start, then move beyond it so the voice becomes your own.
- Before A1 you do an AO1 self-check: your own banding should land within one band of the teacher's. Self-bandings are collected and the gap tracked.
- The holiday splits the unit: pages are built before the break and reviewed after it. Holiday homework is primary-source sketchbook recording.

# 9479 Course Map

> [[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · the whole two-year course at a glance

The course runs **C1 → C3 (across two semesters) → C2**, closing on a **linear A Level entry in June 2028**.

| Semester | When | Component | Cohort | Plan |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | autumn 2026 | C1 Portfolio | G11 | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan\|S1 Plan]] · 5 units |
| S2 | spring 2027 | C3 Personal Investigation, part 1 | G11 | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan\|S2 Plan]] · 5 units |
| S3 | autumn 2027 | C3 part 2 → internal submission | G12 | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-3-plan\|S3 Plan]] · 4 units |
| S4 | spring 2028 | C2 Externally Set Assignment → **all-component submission** | G12 | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan\|S4 Plan]] · 5 units |

**Dates:** S1 follows the [[calendar|2026-27 calendar]]. S2, S3 and S4 dates are provisional and may shift when the school publishes the calendars for spring 2027 and 2027-28.

**What linear entry means here:** all three components are entered in the single June 2028 series, so there is no separate AS award and no carry-forward step. Spring 2027 therefore carries **no Cambridge deadline** — the first and only submission is ≈ end April 2028, where C1, C2 and C3 land together (S4 wk 11).

**Design constants:** the eight-stage portfolio pipeline every semester; 4 Attainments + 8–10 Course Skills tasks per semester; homework every week (sketchbook / written annotation / reflection, ≤30 min); theme control tightens each component — C1 teacher menu → C3 student-proposed and locked in writing → C2 Cambridge-set with the teacher shortlisting 2–3 questions per student.

## S1 — autumn 2026 · C1 Portfolio

| Code | Date | Milestone | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Fri 2026-10-09 | Recording milestone | AO1 |
| A2 | Mon 2026-11-09 | Exploration & artist study | AO2+AO3 |
| A3 | Thu 2026-12-10 | Development | AO3 |
| A4 | Thu 2027-01-07 | Final portfolio | AO1–AO4 |

Full detail: [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|S1 Assessments]].

## S2 — spring 2027 · C3 part 1 (theme → mid-point portfolio)

Opens Mon 2027-02-22. You have three weeks of consultation, then the theme is locked and the research paper drafts alongside the visual work.

| Code | Date | Milestone | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Fri 2027-03-19 | Recording milestone, in C3 specialist language | AO1 |
| A2 | Fri 2027-04-23 | Exploration + Artist 1 | AO2+AO3 |
| A3 | Fri 2027-05-28 | Development + research paper, first full draft | AO3 + integration |
| A4 | Fri 2027-06-11 | **C3 mid-point portfolio** | AO1–AO4 |

Other fixed points: your theme is **locked in writing** by week 3; the Qingming mock week falls early–mid April (sketchbook brief only); the research paper outline is issued mid-semester and its bibliography must be **integrated into the body of work**, not detached (a detached bibliography loses AO1 marks); the semester closes on a **summer production briefing** — the largest uninterrupted C3 window. Full detail: [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|S2 Plan]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s2-assessments|S2 Assessments]].

## S3 — autumn 2027 · C3 part 2

| Code | Date | Milestone | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Thu 2027-10-14 | Development resumed + research paper draft 2 (marked section by section) | AO3 |
| A2 | Thu 2027-10-21 | **Mock portfolio review of the whole investigation** | AO1–AO4 |
| A3 | Mon 2027-11-15 | **Final research paper** | C3 written criteria |
| A4 | Wed 2027-12-22 | **Complete Personal Investigation** — both outcomes + portfolio + paper | AO1–AO4 |

Seniors' Days remove a full week mid-semester, so A2 lands just before it as a whole-investigation mock, and that week is for marking and independent production. Week 1 opens on 1:1 summer-production reviews; the semester ends on assembly, viva-rehearsal critique, and submission QC. Full detail: [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-3-plan|S3 Plan]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s3-assessments|S3 Assessments]].

## S4 — spring 2028 · C2 + submission

| Code | Date | Milestone | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Fri 2028-03-10 | Recording — intention, shoots, sustained studies | AO1 |
| A2 | Fri 2028-03-24 | Experiments + **two artists** + bridge piece + scale trial | AO2+AO3 |
| A3 | Fri 2028-03-31 | Complete supporting studies — **content closes here**; nothing new is taught after | AO3+AO4 |
| A4 | Fri 2028-04-21 | Complete C2 submission package | — |

- **Week 1:** the ESA paper is released and distributed; the teacher shortlists 2–3 questions per student and your **choice is locked by Friday**.
- **The 15-hour supervised test runs in Qingming mock week**, unless the school sets a dedicated window.
- **≈ end April 2028: coursework marks and samples go to Cambridge** — on the linear entry, C1, C2 and C3 all land at this single deadline. The June 2028 timetable publishes ≈ Oct 2027.
- Submit **two artists**: single-artist submissions score in the middle and low bands.

Full detail: [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|S4 Plan]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s4-assessments|S4 Assessments]].

# 9479 S1 Unit 3: Exploration & Artist Study

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 3 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-4-development|Unit 4]] →

## What this unit does

This is the exploration and artist-study stage. The first half covers AO2 (Explore): twelve prescribed media experiments, evidence-based selection, and a scale trial at outcome size. The second half opens AO3: two artist studies chosen to solve — then argue about — your problem, plus a bridge piece that carries the study into development. The unit closes with A2, marked against AO2 and AO3 together.

## Dates & span

Mon 2026-10-12 → Tue 2026-11-10 (W7 → W11), split by the G11 Juniors Days (Wed–Fri of W10). You sit A2 on Mon of W11, and Unit 4 opens mid-week after the feedback day. These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-06-media-exploration|Lesson 06: Media Exploration]] — the twelve prescribed experiments as one three-day run; you decide keep-or-change with evidence.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-07-selection-and-scale|Lesson 07: Selection and Scale]] — critique of the grids, media selected for writable reasons, scale trial (name what broke and repair it — that is your AO2 evidence), Stage 3 page.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-08-artist-study-1|Lesson 08: Artist Study 1]] — from the prescribed pool: analysis → meaning and context → method copy → personal response → influence note.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-09-artist-2-bridge-piece-and-a2|Lesson 09: Artist 2, Bridge Piece and A2]] — Artist 2 chosen to argue with Artist 1; bridge piece finished across the Juniors Days gap; A2 sat; one defensible direction.

## Assessment

**A2, Mon 2026-11-09** — exploration and artist study (twelve experiments, media selection, scale trial, artist pages, bridge piece), marked against AO2+AO3.

CS tasks, each direct practice for A2:
- **CS5** Fri 2026-10-16 — experiments documented.
- **CS6** Fri 2026-10-23 — Stage 3 page + selection note.
- **CS7** Fri 2026-10-30 — artist pages.

See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- **The twelve experiments (prescribed):** ink+stick blind/sighted · charcoal erased back · wet-in-wet watercolor · collage from own photos · monoprint · frottage · wax resist · impasto+scraper · stippling/cross-hatch · three-media layering · low-relief maquette in raking light · digital threshold/overlay. Experiment 11 is not optional: the 2D-to-3D loop is what separates AO2 and AO3 at the top band.
- **Artist pools per theme (prescribed):** Reflections → Estes, Fish, Escher · Worn → Tàpies, Siskind, Kiefer · Hands → Kollwitz, Dürer, Yoshitoshi/craft photographer · Structures → Sheeler, Feininger, Whiteread. Every pool spans period and culture and contains one 3D-capable anchor. Choose an artist who solves your problem, not one you simply like.
- Your one-sentence defensible direction from the A2 feedback day is the entry ticket to Lesson 10: no direction, no composition.

# 9479 S1 Unit 4: Development

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 4 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-5-final-outcome-and-portfolio|Unit 5]] →

## What this unit does

This is the development stage, assessed under AO3 Develop. You build two full compositions — your chosen direction, then a genuine alternative you reject — and critique both against your intention. Targeted tests then answer only what the critique left unresolved. The unit closes by fully planning your final outcome before A3 reviews the whole development trail. AO3 leads into AO4 in the final lesson.

## Dates & span

Wed 2026-11-11 → Fri 2026-12-11 (W11 → W15), around the second G11 Juniors Days block (Wed–Fri of W14). These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-10-composition-1|Lesson 10: Composition 1]] — composed from your own studies using tracing-paper overlays, built over a two-day run, resolved and honestly photographed.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-11-composition-2-and-critique|Lesson 11: Composition 2 and Critique]] — the rejected alternative, built deliberately faster; full-protocol structured critique, after which something changes because of what you heard.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-12-targeted-tests-and-direction|Lesson 12: Targeted Tests and Direction]] — no decoration; your final direction stated with the rejection in full sentences; written evaluation; a Stage 6 page as a decision trail.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-13-final-plan-and-a3|Lesson 13: Final Plan and A3]] — final composition plan, solving thumbnails, palette argued from meaning; A3 sat; the plan amended from feedback.

## Assessment

This unit gates on **A3, Thu 2026-12-10** — a development milestone (compositions 1 and 2, targeted tests, written evaluation, final composition plan) marked against AO3. CS tasks: **CS8** Fri 2026-11-20 (both compositions photographed and laid out) and **CS9** Fri 2026-11-27 (targeted tests + critique response), both direct practice for A3. See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- Keep the one-sentence direction from A2 physically present at your desk. Development that drifts from it is conferenced the same day.
- Two-day builds never restart — carrying a piece through is part of the work. Composition 2's one-day build is intentional pressure, and the process moves faster the second time.
- The rejection paragraph (your final direction, Fri of Lesson 12) carries a lot of weight in the AO4 story.
- Your final plan should be clear enough that a capable stranger could begin the piece from it. Choose palette and materials for meaning and name them as such.
- Documentation matters at submission: a bad photograph loses real marks.

# 9479 S1 Unit 5: Final Outcome & Portfolio

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 5 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s1-unit-4-development|Unit 4]]

## What this unit does

This is the final-outcome and portfolio stage, under AO4 Present. You build your final outcome in one continuous run: four build days, a mid-production review with real time still left, and three resolve days. Submission week then treats assembly as a supervised craft — examiner-grade photography, sequencing your whole journey, an edit with a cut log, and A4, the final portfolio marked across AO1–AO4, which closes the semester.

## Dates & span

Mon 2026-12-14 → Fri 2027-01-08 (W16 → W19), with the winter break (W18) between the declared finish and supervised assembly. The late-work cutoff (around Fri 2027-01-01) falls during the break. These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-14-the-final-outcome|Lesson 14: The Final Outcome]] — four-day build (underdrawing → mid-stages → production → detail), a mid-production review while change is still possible, then a three-day resolve run ending in a written declared finish.
- [[classes/a-level-art-design/lesson-plans/9479-s1-lesson-15-submission-week-and-a4|Lesson 15: Submission Week and A4]] — photograph, sequence, edit with cut log, final AO check, submission Thursday, and a semester reflection with the C3 bridge on Friday.

## Assessment

**A4** is due **Thu 2027-01-07** — the final portfolio (your resolved outcome plus the complete supporting portfolio, sequenced and edited), marked against AO1–AO4. No edits after submission. It is announced Mon 2026-12-21, giving you a 17-day window that covers the break. Grades and comments are returned by Wed 2027-01-20.

One CS task: **CS10** (mid-production review reflection), Fri 2026-12-18 — direct practice for A4. See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s1-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- The build run is the semester's longest and is protected. Any lost day here has no home elsewhere: there are no spare days in the calendar, so attendance matters. Nothing new enters the room during the run.
- The declared finish (Wed 2026-12-23) is real: the break is rest with no homework, and the portfolio is assembled only in the supervised January sessions.
- Submission craft: your photograph is the version the examiner sees; the sequence reads theme → recording → exploration → artists → development → outcome without narration; at least one page is cut for not earning its place, and the cut is logged as evidence of editorial judgment if a mark is queried.
- Friday closes with the C3 bridge: your winter homework is three candidate Personal Investigation themes with annotated references, opening the Semester 2 consultation cycle on Mon 2027-02-22.

# 9479 S2 Unit 2: Recording & Sustained Study

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|Semester 2 Plan]] · Unit 2 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-1-foundations-on-the-investigation|Unit 1]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] →

## What this unit does

This is the recording / sustained-study stage, entirely under AO1 Record, carried in C3 specialist language. You compose recording pages so your thinking is visible to a stranger; you sustain two observed studies, each across a genuine two-day run without restarting; you annotate critically (what the study taught you about the theme, not what happened); and you shoot and annotate your own photographic reference. This unit closes the recording sequence and carries **A1**, the semester's first attainment.

## Dates & span

Weeks S4–S5 (Mon 2027-03-14 → ~2027-03-27), 10 sessions. These spring dates are provisional and may shift once the school publishes the calendar for that period; see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|semester plan]].

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| U2·L1 | Compose the Stage 1 page | Thinking visible to a stranger |
| U2·L2–L3 | Studio: sustained study 1 (2-day session) | One study sustained, not restarted |
| U2·L4 | Critical annotation | What the study taught about the theme |
| U2·L5 | **A1: Recording milestone** | Everything recorded so far, marked against AO1 |
| U2·L6 | Feedback and targets | Three specific actions from the feedback |
| U2·L7–L8 | Studio: sustained study 2 (2-day session) | Pushed past the first |
| U2·L9 | Photography and annotation | Own reference, annotated for what is seen |
| U2·L10 | Studio: build the recording pages | Sequenced so AO1 reads without explanation |

## Assessment

**A1, Fri 2027-03-19** — recording milestone (approved intention, Stage 1 pages, your first sustained study, supporting recording), marked against AO1 with C3 specialist language in view. One CS task inside the unit: **CS3** Fri 2027-03-26 (recording pages build check, consolidating your A1 feedback actions). See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s2-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- Both sustained studies must show duration — layered decisions, not single sittings.
- Descriptive annotation caps your AO1 mark; **critical reflection on progress is the top-band behavior**.
- All reference is self-generated: nothing found, nothing borrowed.
- Convert your A1 feedback into three named actions and act on them visibly by CS3.

# 9479 S2 Unit 1: Foundations on the Investigation

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|Semester 2 Plan]] · Unit 1 of 5 · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]] →

## What this unit does

This unit opens the foundations stage of Component 3. You read a 90/100 investigation and name what earns the marks, then propose three candidate themes and defend them one to one; each is screened against primary sources you can actually reach and against all eight pipeline stages. You lock one theme in writing — you propose it, and the teacher approves it against the exemplar criteria — and the unit closes with a statement of intent in your own words, a research plan, and your first primary-source shoot. Once locked, the theme is fixed: your investigation runs on it to submission next December.

## Dates & span

Weeks S1–S3 (Mon 2027-02-21 → ~2027-03-13), 15 sessions. These spring dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for that period — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|semester plan]].

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| U1·L1 | C3 briefing and the marks | How the investigation is marked (C3 is weighted 75 marks → 150); what the written element must do |
| U1·L2 | Reading strong investigations | Name what earns the marks in an exemplar |
| U1·L3 | Theme longlist from the winter homework | Three candidates, with references |
| U1·L4 | Consultation round 1, one to one | Defend each candidate to the teacher |
| U1·L5 | Feasibility screens | Test candidates against reachable sources |
| U1·L6 | Proposal drafting: theme and aims | Why this theme can sustain sixteen pages |
| U1·L7 | Primary source plan | Shoots and sittings you control |
| U1·L8 | Artist shortlist | Artists who solve the problem, not favourites |
| U1·L9 | Consultation round 2, one to one | A decision or a fix |
| U1·L10 | Feasibility test against the pipeline | Walk all eight stages; fix what breaks |
| U1·L11 | Theme locked in writing | Signed with the teacher; no restarts after |
| U1·L12 | Statement of intent finalised | The investigation in your own words |
| U1·L13 | Research plan | Reading, sources and dates |
| U1·L14 | First primary source shoot | Reference you will draw from for months |
| U1·L15 | Shoot review and annotation | Annotate what is seen, not what is felt |

## Assessment

There are no attainment gates in this unit. The first, **A1**, sits Fri 2027-03-19 in [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]]. CS tasks: **CS1** Fri 2027-03-05 (proposal draft — theme, aims, source plan, artist shortlist), **CS2** Fri 2027-03-12 (statement of intent + annotated references). See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s2-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- **The primary-source plan contains zero found imagery** — "nothing found, nothing borrowed" is the C3 standard, as in S1.
- The artist shortlist is chosen for problems solved, not personal taste.
- The statement of intent is in your own words and names what will be investigated and why.

# 9479 S2 Unit 3: Exploration & Artist Study

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|Semester 2 Plan]] · Unit 3 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-2-recording-and-sustained-study|Unit 2]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-4-development|Unit 4]] →

## What this unit does

This is the exploration and artist-study stage. For AO2 (Explore) you run twelve prescribed media experiments, then make an evidence-based selection, naming the media you reject. AO3 opens in the back half: you study two artists closely — analysis, meaning and context, method, and personal response — until that study visibly changes your own work, and you make a bridge piece and a scale trial. The contextual writing you produce here is the first material of your research paper. The unit closes with **A2**, marked across AO2 and AO3.

## Dates & span

Weeks S6, S8–S9, around the S7 mock (Mon 2027-03-28 → about 2027-04-24), 15 sessions. These spring dates are provisional and may shift when the school publishes the calendar; see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|semester plan]].

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| U3·L1 | Plan the media exploration | Experiments that could fail |
| U3·L2–L4 | Studio: media experiments 1 to 12 (3-day session) | Which media carry the investigation |
| U3·L5 | Keep or change | Evidence-based selection; name your rejections |
| *S7 mock* | No new content | Independent sketchbook brief if the school permits |
| U3·L6 | Artist 1: choose and analyze | An artist who solves the problem |
| U3·L7 | Meaning and context | First material of the research paper |
| U3·L8 | Method study | Copy to understand, not to own |
| U3·L9 | Studio: personal response | Answer the artist, do not imitate |
| U3·L10 | Studio: influence note and page | Influence shown in the work |
| U3·L11 | Artist 2 analysis and bridge plan | A second angle set against the first |
| U3·L12 | Studio: make the bridge piece | From study into development |
| U3·L13 | Studio: scale trial | Fix what the scale broke |
| U3·L14 | Studio: lay out the Stage 3 pages | Exploration as reasoning |
| U3·L15 | **A2: Exploration and artist study** | The whole unit, marked together |

## Assessment

**A2, Fri 2027-04-23** — exploration and artist study (twelve experiments, media selection, artist pages, bridge piece, Stage 3 pages), marked against AO2+AO3.

Coursework tasks:
- **CS4**, Fri 2027-04-02 — twelve experiments documented
- **CS5**, Fri 2027-04-16 — artist 1 pages (analysis, method, response); opens the research paper material

See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s2-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- Your media choices must carry conceptual logic: the selection argument is what earns AO2 at the top band.
- Your artist references must change the work on the page — citation without a response earns nothing.

S7 carries no new content, and the twelve experiments close before the mock window.

# 9479 S2 Unit 4: Development

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|Semester 2 Plan]] · Unit 4 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-5-mid-point-portfolio|Unit 5]] →

## What this unit does

This is the development stage, under AO3 Develop, with the research paper drafted alongside it. You build two competing compositions to the same resolved standard — the direction you choose and the alternative you genuinely reject — and critique both against your intention. Targeted tests answer only what the critique left unresolved. You then choose the final direction, write out why you rejected the other, and assemble the written evaluation and the Stage 6 page as a record of your decisions. You also complete **draft 1** of the research paper from your contextual writing, following a teacher-set outline. The unit closes with **A3**, marked against AO3.

## Dates & span

Weeks S10–S14 (Mon 2027-04-25 → ~2027-05-29), 22 sessions, including the reduced May Day week (S11). These spring dates are provisional and may shift when the school publishes the calendar for that period.

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| U4·L1 | Feedback; compose composition 1 | From your own studies |
| U4·L2–L3 | Studio: build composition 1 (2-day session) | One idea held across two days |
| U4·L4 | Research paper outline issued | Teacher structure, your content |
| U4·L5 | Studio: push composition 1 | As far as it goes before the break |
| U4·L6 | Studio: resolve composition 1 | Unresolved work cannot be judged |
| U4·L7 | Photograph and lay out | Honest documentation |
| U4·L8 | Studio: compose composition 2 | The rejected alternative, tried |
| U4·L9–L10 | Studio: build composition 2 (2-day session) | Equal effort for the second idea |
| U4·L11 | Studio: resolve composition 2 | A fair comparison |
| U4·L12 | Research paper sections drafted | Sections against the outline |
| U4·L13 | Structured critique | Both compositions against the intention |
| U4·L14 | Studio: act on the critique | Change something because of what you heard |
| U4·L15–L16 | Studio: targeted tests (2-day session) | Only what is unresolved |
| U4·L17 | Studio: choose the final direction | The rejection written out |
| U4·L18 | Studio: write the evaluation | Against the intention, not the effort |
| U4·L19 | Studio: assemble the Stage 6 page | A readable trail of decisions |
| U4·L20 | Research paper draft 1 completed | Sections into one full draft |
| U4·L21 | Studio: draft repair and gaps | Fix what the full draft exposed |
| U4·L22 | **A3: Development milestone** | Development plus draft 1, marked together |

## Assessment

**A3, Fri 2027-05-28** — the development milestone plus research paper draft 1 (compositions 1 and 2, targeted tests, evaluation, Stage 6 page), marked against AO3. This is the last session before the 14 days leading to A4.

CS tasks:
- **CS6** Thu 2027-04-29 — paper outline populated
- **CS7** Fri 2027-05-14 — paper section drafts
- **CS8** Fri 2027-05-21 — targeted tests + critique response

See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s2-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- Both compositions must reach the same resolved standard, so the comparison is fair; composition 2 is not built to fail.

# 9479 S2 Unit 5: Mid-Point Portfolio

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-2-plan|Semester 2 Plan]] · Unit 5 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s2-unit-4-development|Unit 4]]

## What this unit does

The mid-point portfolio stage, under AO1–AO4 Present. **A4, Fri 2027-06-11** — the mid-point portfolio (recording, exploration, artist pages, development, one resolved piece and the research paper draft, sequenced and edited), marked against AO1–AO4; no edits after submission. - Homework stays capped at 30 minutes: exam pace does not mean more homework.

# 9479 S4 Unit 3: Exploration & Artist Study

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|Semester 4 Plan]] · Unit 3 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-2-recording|Unit 2]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-4-development|Unit 4]] →

## What this unit does

This is the exploration and artist-study stage at exam pace, across AO2 Explore and AO3 Develop. You turn your A1 feedback into targets; run **eight** experiments (drawn from selections you have already proven) to a written keep-or-change decision; analyse two artists and answer them in visible work within single sessions; and produce a bridge piece and a scale trial — at the supervised-test dimensions — to carry your study into development. The unit closes with **A2**, marked across AO2 and AO3.

## Dates & span

Weeks S4–S5 (about 2028-03-12 → 2028-03-25), 10 sessions. These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| U3·L1 | Feedback; plan the experiments | Targets first, then risk |
| U3·L2–L3 | Studio: media experiments (2-day session) | Eight experiments; what carries the question |
| U3·L4 | Artist 1: analysis and response in one pass | Analyse, then answer in work |
| U3·L5 | Artist 1 response work | Influence in the work |
| U3·L6 | Artist 2: analysis and response | Two angles, one question |
| U3·L7 | Studio: bridge piece | From study into development |
| U3·L8 | Studio: scale trial | At the test scale; fix what broke |
| U3·L9 | Exploration pages | Reasoning, not a swatch chart |
| U3·L10 | **A2: Exploration and artist study** | The whole unit, marked together |

## Assessment

**A2 — Fri 2028-03-24:** exploration and artist study (experiments, media selection, two artists with visible work responses, bridge piece, scale trial), marked against AO2+AO3.

CS tasks:
- **CS4** — Fri 2028-03-17: experiment documentation + artist 1 response
- **CS5** — Tue 2028-03-21: artist pages + bridge piece check

See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s4-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- ◆ Your media choices carry conceptual logic **even at exam pace**.
- ◆ Citation without response earns nothing — **the response is the evidence**.
- The scale trial happens at the dimensions the supervised test will use: surprises belong here, not in the test room.

# 9479 S4 Unit 4: Development

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|Semester 4 Plan]] · Unit 4 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study|Unit 3]] | [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-5-supervised-test-and-submission|Unit 5]] →

## What this unit does

This is the development stage, under AO3 Develop closing into AO4 Present — the tightest unit in the course. You compose your final-piece candidates from your own studies (the test will realise one of them); you compare them on a **draft comparison page** and write out why you reject the others; you fix a final composition plan so nothing has to be invented under supervision; and you complete every supporting study and decide the mounted order. **Content closes at A3** — nothing new is taught after it.

## Dates & span

Week S6 plus the elastic window (2028-03-26 → 2028-03-31 for the coded sessions; the S7 window can add up to four days on reading B). These S4 dates are projected and may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|semester plan]].

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| U4·L1 | Feedback; compose the final piece candidates | From your own studies |
| U4·L2 | Draft comparison page | Rejections articulated in writing |
| U4·L3 | Final composition plan | Nothing invented under supervision |
| U4·L4 | Supporting studies completed | Every gap closed |
| U4·L5 | **A3: Complete supporting studies — content close** | Comparison page, plan, mounted order. |
| *Elastic (S7)* | Reading B window | All four teaching days go to refinement and rehearsal |

## Assessment

**A3, Fri 2028-03-31** — complete supporting studies (draft comparison page with articulated rejections, final composition plan, mounted order), marked against AO3+AO4. This is the content close: nothing new is taught after it.

One CS task: **CS6**, Thu 2028-03-30 (composition critique response + draft comparison page).

See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s4-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- ◆ **The draft comparison page with articulated rejections earns top AO4** — it is the center of this unit.
- Your final plan should be complete enough that the test is execution, not invention.
- This unit is compressed into one taught development week, widened only by the elastic window. Every day here counts, so attendance matters.

# 9479 S4 Unit 5: Supervised Test & Submission

[[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|Semester 4 Plan]] · Unit 5 of 5 · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-4-development|Unit 4]]

## What this unit does

This is the test, assembly and board stage, under AO4 Present. You realise your planned outcome inside a **15-hour supervised test** under exam conditions: work is stored securely between sittings, with no access and no additions. You then photograph the final piece and assemble the submission package in the exact order the Cambridge specification requires, check it line by line under supervision, and hand it over for marking. **C1, C2 and C3 all reach the board in the same window.** The unit closes with **A4** — the submission package, which is your internal grade layer; the final piece itself is marked externally by Cambridge.

## Dates & span

Weeks S7/S8–S10: the 15-hour test plus 10 sessions. The test is scheduled for S8, **Mon 2028-04-10 → Fri 04-14 (5 × 3h)**. An alternative timing around Qingming in S7 (4 × 3h45) would pull the whole tail forward one week; this will be confirmed in class. **All S4 dates are projected** and may shift when the school publishes its calendar for this period — see the [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-4-plan|semester plan]].

## Sequence of work

| Code | Session | Focus |
|---|---|---|
| Test | The supervised test, 15 hours | The plan realised; nothing invented under supervision |
| U5·L1 | Test debrief and package plan | What the test produced |
| U5·L2 | Photograph the final piece | The version the examiner sees |
| U5·L3 | Assemble the submission package | Ordered exactly as specified |
| U5·L4 | Quality control, supervised | The specification, line by line |
| U5·L5 | **A4: Submission package due** | The internal grade layer; Cambridge marks the piece |
| U5·L6 | Gap repair for the board, supervised | Only what the QC named |
| U5·L7 | Final checks against the specification | Every page, label and limit |
| U5·L8 | Handover for marking | The checklist covers C1, C2 and C3 |
| U5·L9 | Portfolio photography for records | The record survives the submission |
| U5·L10 | Course close and release | The board has it |

## Assessment

**A4, Fri 2028-04-21** (floats with the test): the complete C2 submission package, your internal grade layer, marked against AO1–AO4. No edits after this point. **The final C2 piece itself is marked externally by Cambridge.** CS tasks: **CS7** Thu 2028-04-20 (submission checklist and reflection, floats with the test); **CS8** Thu 2028-04-27 (portfolio photography for your own records). See the [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s4-assessments|full register]].

## Key content

- ◆ **Nothing is invented under supervision** — the plan marked at A3 is both your safety and your mark.
- Storage between sittings is secure and documented: no access, no additions, no exceptions.
- The package matches the specification exactly, and marks and samples reach the board by the end-April deadline.
- On the earlier timing, the whole unit pulls forward one week.
