classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciatio.body:
# Art Appreciation Resource Library

## Readings — assigned

| Reading | Where it lands |
|---|---|
| **John Berger, "The Eaters and the Eaten"** | The anchor of the food strand. Its opening line — *"To eat is to appropriate, to take into ourselves the lives of others"* — heads the Food and Ethics unit. Serves U3 |
| **John Berger, *Ways of Seeing*, ch. 1** | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-10-berger-and-the-reproduction\|L10 Berger and the reproduction]] |
| **Walter Benjamin, "The Work of Art in the Age of Its Technological Reproducibility" (2nd version, 1936/2008)** | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-11-aura-and-the-poor-image\|L11 Aura and the poor image]] — the aura half |
| **Hito Steyerl, "In Defense of the Poor Image"** | L11 — the poor-image half. The two readings are read against each other |
| **Susan Sontag, "In Plato's Cave"** | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-12-photography-and-after\|L12 Photography and after]]. This is *On Photography*'s first essay, extracted — the full book is on the reference shelf |
| **Dean Kissick, "The Painted Protest"** · **"Hello Darkness"** | Contemporary art-world criticism; fits [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-13-the-artworld-and-the-brief\|L13 The artworld and the brief]] |

## Further reading

| Reading | Note |
|---|---|
| **Michael Baxandall, *Painting and Experience in Fifteenth-Century Italy*** | The source of "painting is a deposit of a social relationship." Underpins the patronage strand |
| **Francis Haskell, *Patrons and Painters: Art and Society in Baroque Italy*** | Baroque patronage. A 25-page extract (pp. 120–145) is the teaching text; the full book is the shelf copy |
| **Sarah Thornton, *Seven Days in the Art World*** | Ethnography of the contemporary art world — the empirical counterpart to Danto's philosophy at L13 |
| **Susan Sontag, *On Photography*** (full) | The complete book; "In Plato's Cave" is its first essay |
| **John Berger, *Portraits*** | |
| **The Image Object Post-Internet** | Post-internet art theory; pairs with the Ulman and Horvitz videos |
| **Documents of Contemporary Art** — 16 volumes: Abstraction · Appropriation · Beauty · Documentary · Failure · Nature · Networks · Sound · Systems · The Archive · The Artist's Joke · The Cute · The Everyday · The Sublime · Time · Utopias | The Whitechapel/MIT anthology series. A **thematic shelf, not a syllabus** — each volume is an edited collection of short primary texts. Use it to source one or two excerpts when your Atlas Book theme needs theory |

The DOCA series is the most reusable resource here for the **Atlas Book capstone**: if you are working on masks and identity, or screens, or ruins, you can go to a volume rather than open search.

# Art Appreciation

> **Start here:** [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-2-outline|Semester 2 Outline]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|S1 Assessments]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation-resource-library|Reading list]]

This is an **elective, internally assessed** course at BNDS — no syllabus code, no external paper. It is AP-derived in ancestry and runs under the BNDS A-Level Handbook rules ([[shared/bnds-assessment-framework]]). The class is a **mixed G11 + G12 group of about 11 students** in an ESL room. The critique protocol is adapted: you write before speaking.

**Method:** "Aby Warburg's Mnemosyne Atlas: thematic and non-linear, not chronological. Units are constellations, not periods. The throughline is that the structures of the art world are accumulated conventions rather than natural laws." The three constellation assessments (A1 board → A4 synthesis → End-of-Term retrospective) are the same move at increasing scale.

**Teaching pattern:** Mon P4 (45') · Tue P6+7 (DOUBLE, 90') · Wed P1 (45') · Fri P2 (45') — four teaching days, five periods, one double, no Thursday. The four in-class attainments (essays and constellation builds) run on the Tuesday doubles.

## Semester 1

Five units across Autumn 2026-27 — detail in the [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]]:

| Unit | Weeks | Theme |
|---|---|---|
| U1 | W1–3 | Origins and Ancestral Memory (caves, survival, ritual objects, the first board) |
| U2 | W4–7 | Narrative, Myth and Allegory (storyboards; Gentileschi; **Titian vs Manet**) |
| U3 | W8–12 | Food, the Table and Ethics (*Eat Drink Man Woman*; Heda; Last Supper; Qingming scroll; Cassatt/Rosler/Weems) |
| U4 | W13–17 | Images, Mediation and Modernity (Berger; ukiyo-e; Benjamin; Steyerl; Sontag; Greenberg; Danto) |
| U5 | W19 | Semester close: the constellation retrospective (W18 is the winter break) |

**Theorist spine in teaching order: Warburg → Berger → Benjamin → Steyerl → Sontag → Greenberg → Danto.**

Major assessments:
- A1 board — Fri 2026-09-18
- A2 Titian-vs-Manet in-class essay — Tue 2026-10-13
- A3 food/table/ethics essay — Tue 2026-11-17
- A4 synthesis constellation — Tue 2026-12-15
- End-of-Term retrospective — Tue 2027-01-05 (20%)

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for that period; any change will be confirmed in class.

Semester 2 is a titled 16-week outline (art market, patronage, *La Chimera*, Shanzhai, Atlas Book capstone) — [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-2-outline|Semester 2 Outline]].

classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/94.body:
# 9479 Semester 4 Plan

> [[classes/a-level-art-design/a-level-art-design|A Level Art & Design]] · Semester 4 of 4 ([[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-course-map|course map]]) · ← [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-semester-3-plan|S3 C3 part 2]] · course closes

**Component 2 (Externally Set Assignment) plus all-component submission, G12, spring 2027–28.** One session every teaching day, from Mon 2028-02-21 to the content close on Fri 2028-03-31, then the mock window, the 15-hour supervised test, assembly and the board handover: **40 coded sessions** (30 in the six-week core, 10 in assembly/board) plus the elastic window and the test.

The dates below (weeks S1–S12, and A1–A4) are projected and may shift when the school publishes the spring 2027–28 calendar. A4 and the supervised test move with the mock window.

## The five units

| Unit | Weeks | Gate |
|---|---|---|
| [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-1-foundations-on-the-question\|U1 Foundations on the Question]] | S1 | question locked in writing |
| [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-2-recording\|U2 Recording]] | S2–S3 | **A1** Fri 2028-03-10 |
| [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-3-exploration-and-artist-study\|U3 Exploration & Artist Study]] | S4–S5 | **A2** Fri 2028-03-24 |
| [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-4-development\|U4 Development]] | S6 + elastic window | **A3** Fri 2028-03-31 (content close) |
| [[classes/a-level-art-design/unit-plans/9479-s4-unit-5-supervised-test-and-submission\|U5 Supervised Test & Submission]] | S7/S8–S10 (floats) | **A4** Fri 2028-04-21 (floats with the test) |

Full register: [[classes/a-level-art-design/assessments/9479-s4-assessments|9479 S4 Assessments]].

## Week by week

| Wk | Dates | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| S1 | 02-20 → 26 | U1: ESA paper analyzed; question matched to your proven strengths and **locked in writing by Fri 02-25**; CS1 |
| S2 | 02-27 → 03-04 | U2 recording at exam pace: intention + shoot plan; own reference shot + drawn (2-day); sustained study opens; CS2 |
| S3 | 03-05 → 11 | Sustained study (2-day); critical annotation Thu 03-09; **A1 Fri 03-10** — recording (AO1); CS3 Thu 03-09 |
| S4 | 03-12 → 18 | U3: eight experiments (2-day); artist 1 analyzed + answered in work Fri 03-17; CS4 |
| S5 | 03-19 → 25 | Artist 2 + bridge piece + scale trial; exploration pages; **A2 Fri 03-24** (AO2+AO3); CS5 Tue 03-21 |
| S6 | 03-26 → 04-01 | U4: compose candidates; draft comparison page; final composition plan; **A3 Fri 03-31** — content close (AO3+AO4) same day |
| S7 | 04-02 → 08 | **Mock window (floats).** The 15-hour supervised test may run this week, as 4 sittings of 3h45 around Qingming (Tue 04-04); otherwise these four days go to U4 refinement/rehearsal. The exact schedule is confirmed in class. |
| S8 | 04-09 → 15 | **The 15-hour supervised test** — if it runs this week, 5 sittings × 3h, Mon–Fri; work is stored securely between sittings |
| S9 | 04-16 → 22 | U5: photograph the final piece; assemble + supervised QC of the package; **A4 Fri 04-21** — submission package (AO1–AO4), no edits; CS7 Thu 04-20 |
| S10 | 04-23 → 29 | Board week: handover (C1+C2+C3 together); marks & samples ≈ end April; records photography; CS8 Thu 04-27 |
| S11 | 04-30 → 05-06 | **May Day full week (Mon 05-01 → Fri 05-05)** — no teaching; reflection homework only |
| S12 | 05-07 → 13 | Close: two-year review; portfolio return (everything the board does not hold); A2-season briefing — **Reduced** |

classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art.body:
# Art Appreciation S1 Assessments

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Autumn 2026–27

## Weights

Under the [[shared/bnds-assessment-framework|BNDS A-Level assessment framework]]: **Attainment 50%** (A1–A4) · **Course Skills 20%** (CS1–5) · **Learning Behaviour 10%** (LB postings) · **End of Term 20%** (the retrospective). This course has only four attainment items, so no attainment mark is dropped (dropping requires at least six).

## Majors (A1–A4 + End of Term)

| Item | You sit | What is marked | Format |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A1 | Fri 2026-09-18 | Unit 1 board + justification — close looking, justified adjacency | Group board built on the wall + individual written justification |
| A2 | Tue 2026-10-13 | Unit 2 comparative essay — one claim about Titian vs Manet, evidenced from the pictures | In-class essay, closed |
| A3 | Tue 2026-11-17 | Unit 3 essay — food, the table and ethics, argued from the pictures | In-class essay |
| A4 | Tue 2026-12-15 | Unit 4 synthesis — a constellation that argues across the whole unit | In-class constellation build + written argument |
| EoT | Tue 2027-01-05 | Semester-close retrospective — a semester of looking as one argument, in the layout | In-class constellation, 20% End of Term |

Your mid-term grades (issued Thu 2026-10-29) are based on A2 and participation.

## Course Skills (CS1–CS5)

Each CS rehearses the major next to it. You get at least 72 hours' notice, and marks come back within 5 working days.

| Item | Date | What | Rehearses |
|---|---|---|---|
| CS1 | Fri 2026-09-04 | Darkroom reflection, written in the room | A1 (image as physical trace → U4) |
| CS2 | Tue 2026-09-29 | Ten-frame storyboard, no captions, no dialogue | A2 (reading what a picture decided) |
| CS3 | Fri 2026-10-09 | Storyboard debrief — where the sequence stopped being legible | A2 |
| CS4 | Fri 2026-11-13 | Heda paragraph — every adjective earned | A3 |
| CS5 | Wed 2026-12-09 | Poor-image lab — degrade an image and describe exactly what changed | A4 |

## Learning Behaviour (LB1–LB9)

Participation postings, graded on behaviour (taking a position and moving it), not performance.

| Item | Date | Session |
|---|---|---|
| LB1 | Fri 2026-09-11 | U1 seminar: what the first images wanted |
| LB2 | Wed 2026-09-23 | U2 cycle-reading session |
| LB3 | Fri 2026-10-23 | U3 seminar: food, class, who is not in the picture |
| LB4 | Fri 2026-10-30 | U3 hospitality seminar — posted for whoever is present (G12 away, not penalised) |
| LB5 | Tue 2026-11-03 | Second Heda session |
| LB6 | Wed 2026-11-25 | Reproduction seminar |
| LB7 | Tue 2026-12-01 | Floating-world session |
| LB8 | Tue 2026-12-22 | Danto/Warhol day — End-of-Semester Activity, short room |
| LB9 | Tue 2027-01-19 | Return days — **carries no S1 weight**; it counts toward S2 |

classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/ar.body:
# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 03: The First Board

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-1-origins-and-ancestral-memory|Unit 1]] · Lesson 03 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-02-what-survives-and-why|L02]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-04-telling-it-without-words|L04]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U1·L10`–`U1·L14` |
| Days | 5 |
| Dates | Mon 2026-09-14 to Fri 2026-09-18 |
| Deliverable | A1: Unit 1 board and justification |
| Homework | Gather candidate images, 30 min/night, days 1–3 |

## Goal

The board is the course in miniature, and this is the first time you build one. A mood board with "obvious" adjacencies is not enough — the justification is what matters, and it is judged hard.

Do not start arranging before you have enough candidate images. If you begin early, you only arrange what you happen to have.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 14 Sept · `U1·L10`** — Neolithic: settlement and the made object. What changes in images when people stop moving.
- **Day 2 · Tue 15 Sept · `U1·L11`** — ritual objects and ancestral presence: what an object *does*, not only what it depicts.
- **Day 3 · Wed 16 Sept · `U1·L12`** — constellation workshop: two unlike images, one defended pairing, out loud, everybody. Direct practice for A1.
- **Day 4 · Thu 17 Sept · `U1·L13`** — build the Unit 1 board on the wall; nothing fixed today.
- **Day 5 · Fri 18 Sept · `U1·L14`** — final hang (20 min), justification written (20 min). **A1 collected. Boards stay up.**

## Assessment

- **You sit A1 on Fri 2026-09-18** — Unit 1 board and justification. The criteria sheet is issued with the announcement on Fri 2026-09-04, not on the day. You are marked on: every adjacency justified with a reason that could not be moved to a different pair; the argument legible from the layout before a word is read; objects described by what they do.
- The grade is half collective: a group board plus your individual write-up.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 02: What Survives, and Why

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-1-origins-and-ancestral-memory|Unit 1]] · Lesson 02 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-01-opening-the-atlas|L01]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-03-the-first-board|L03]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U1·L4`–`U1·L9` |
| Days | 6 |
| Dates | Fri 2026-09-04 to Fri 2026-09-11 |
| Deliverable | one-page panel reading, plus LB1 |
| Feeds | A1 |
| Homework | Chauvet reading, 30 min, set day 2, due day 4 |

## Goal

This lesson covers people who left no writing. You have to argue from the wall itself: **the wall is the evidence.** A1 is announced on the first day of this lesson.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Fri 4 Sept · `U1·L4`** — A1 is announced in the first ten minutes: the board, the justification, due Fri 18 Sept. Then: what survives, and why — name what had to happen for an object to reach you at all.
- **Day 2 · Mon 7 Sept · `U1·L5`** — Lascaux and Chauvet, first pass, no commentary; describe round. Chauvet reading set.
- **Day 3 · Tue 8 Sept · `U1·L6`** — hands, animals and the dark: account for the choice of animal, wall, and depth.
- **Day 4 · Wed 9 Sept · `U1·L7`** — why make a mark: one reason argued, evidenced from the wall.
- **Day 5 · Thu 10 Sept · `U1·L8`** — read a cave wall the way Warburg read a panel, by proximity. Two paired images; the written panel reading is the deliverable.
- **Day 6 · Fri 11 Sept · `U1·L9`** — seminar: what the first images wanted. Thirty minutes; LB1 posted from what happens inside it.

## Assessment

- **A1** — announced Fri 2026-09-04, due Fri 2026-09-18.
- **LB1** — Fri 2026-09-11, posted from the seminar. Graded on whether you take a position and move it — that behavior is what is being watched.
- **Deliverable**: the one-page panel reading (day 5) — direct rehearsal for the A1 justification, the same move at greater length. This will be confirmed in class.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 01: Opening the Atlas

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-1-origins-and-ancestral-memory|Unit 1]] · Lesson 01 of 15 · [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-02-what-survives-and-why|L02]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U1·L1`–`U1·L3` |
| Days | 3 |
| Dates | Tue 2026-09-01 to Fri 2026-09-04 |
| Deliverable | CS1 darkroom reflection |
| Feeds | A1 |

These dates fall in a future term and may shift once the school publishes the calendar for that period.

## Goal

Three days on one job: looking before explaining. The atlas method is different from art history taught as a timeline, and the course uses it deliberately. The darkroom comes on day three because it is the fastest way to make an image stop being a screen and become a thing that light did to a surface — a distinction the course returns to in Unit 4 in December.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Tue 1 Sept · `U1·L1`** — **First half:** course opener — introductions, why this subject, and how the course runs. This is a mixed G11 + G12 group. **Second half:** the atlas method and how you are marked — Warburg's panels and why resemblance beats date, and the course map showing the four majors and the retrospective on one sheet. To close, you name one thing about this course that sounds wrong to you.
- **Day 2 · Wed 2 Sept · `U1·L2`** — look before you explain. Ten minutes silent on one image with the title withheld; write a description for two minutes, then say it aloud. To close, note what someone else saw that you did not.
- **Day 3 · Thu 3 Sept · `U1·L3`** — darkroom: the image as physical trace. Twenty-five minutes making, fifteen minutes writing the reflection while the print is still wet. CS1 is collected at the door.

## Assessment

- **CS1** — darkroom reflection, written in the room, collected Fri 2026-09-04. Marked on naming a physical decision rather than a feeling about the result.
- No homework this first week.
- A1 is announced on the first day of Lesson 02 (Fri 2026-09-04), not during this lesson.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 06: The Table as Moral Space

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics|Unit 3]] · Lesson 06 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-05-titian-to-manet|L05]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-07-the-split-weeks|L07]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U3·L1`, `U3·L2-3` (two-day run), `U3·L4`, `U3·L5` |
| Days | 5 |
| Dates | Mon 2026-10-19 to Fri 2026-10-23 |
| Deliverable | A written reading of one meal, plus LB3 |
| Feeds | A3 |
| Homework | Finish the film on your own if the screening runs short; otherwise none |

## Goal

Unit 3 opens on a film because the table is easier to see when it is moving. In Ang Lee's cooking scenes, everything the family cannot say is said through food. You will watch how the noticing works before you take apart the structure. The screening runs across Tuesday and Wednesday as one continuing session.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 19 Oct · `U3·L1`** — the table as moral space, the frame for the whole unit; first look at three paintings of meals: who is where.
- **Days 2–3 · Tue 20 – Wed 21 Oct · `U3·L2-3`** — *Eat Drink Man Woman* screening as one two-day session: day one screens 40 minutes; day two screens 45 more, picking up on the cut.
- **Thu 22 Oct · `U3·L4`** — the film's table: one meal replayed twice, then the written reading — "an argument nobody can make aloud." This reading is your deliverable.
- **Fri 23 Oct · `U3·L5`** — seminar: food, class, and who is not in the picture. LB3 posted.

The dates above may shift when the school publishes its calendar for this period.

## Assessment

- **LB3** — Fri 2026-10-23, seminar posting. This day may be used for the Autumn Field Trip instead; the final date will be confirmed in class.
- **Deliverable**: a one-page written reading of a single meal sequence (Thu), based on the `U3·L4` goal.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 04: Telling It Without Words

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-2-narrative-myth-and-allegory|Unit 2]] · Lesson 04 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-03-the-first-board|L03]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-05-titian-to-manet|L05]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U2·L1`–`U2·L8` |
| Days | 8 — the longest lesson of the semester |
| Dates | Sun 2026-09-20 to Wed 2026-09-30 |
| Template | MAKE |
| Deliverable | CS2 ten-frame storyboard, set as break homework |
| Feeds | A2 |
| Homework | Break homework: the ten-frame sequence, 3–4 h across the twelve-day holiday, set on day 8 |

## Goal

Eight days ending on the last session before a twelve-day holiday. Your storyboard is the break homework, and the A2 brief goes out with it on day 8, so day 8 matters — miss it and you miss the A2 brief. Five days build the vocabulary (narrative, myth, allegory, cycle); the last three turn it into something you make.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Sun 20 Sept · `U2·L1`** — the critique flow taught explicitly and cold: describe, account, judge, run on an image nobody has seen. This is a make-up day.
- **Day 2 · Mon 21 Sept · `U2·L2`** — what a narrative image has to solve: find where a still image puts time.
- **Day 3 · Tue 22 Sept · `U2·L3`** — myth as shared shorthand; what happens when the audience does not know.
- **Day 4 · Wed 23 Sept · `U2·L4`** — allegory: separate what is shown from what it stands in for.
- **Day 5 · Thu 24 Sept · `U2·L5`** — reading a cycle across panels, not inside one. LB2 posted.
- **Day 6 · Mon 28 Sept · `U2·L6`** — storyboarding without words: one story, three framings.
- **Day 7 · Tue 29 Sept · `U2·L7`** — make the ten frames; swap and read a stranger's. CS2 collected.
- **Day 8 · Wed 30 Sept · `U2·L8`** — A2 announced first (Titian against Manet, Thu 15 Oct), then the break homework set: the sequence, 3–4 hours, no more.

## Assessment

- **LB2** — Wed 2026-09-23, posting from the cycle session.
- **CS2** — Tue 2026-09-29, a ten-frame storyboard a stranger can read in the right order, no captions, no dialogue.
- **A2** — announced Wed 2026-09-30, debriefed as CS3 on Fri 2026-10-09 in lesson 05.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 07: The Split Weeks

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics|Unit 3]] · Lesson 07 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-06-the-table-as-moral-space|L06]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-08-heda-and-the-image-of-work|L08]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U3·L6`–`U3·L10` |
| Days | 5, each complete in itself |
| Dates | Mon 2026-10-26 to Fri 2026-10-30 |
| Deliverable | The reflection on your own table, plus LB4 |
| Feeds | A3 |
| Homework | None |

## About this week

G12 is at Seniors' Day all week, so nothing carries from one day to the next and nothing assessable sits here. These are five sessions that do not build on each other. The through-line is thematic, not sequential — five different ways into the same question, any of which works on its own. If you miss all five, you have missed content, not a running argument.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 26 Oct · `U3·L6`** — shared-lunch prep: the meal as artwork, planned in pairs, with your decisions defended rather than just preferred.
- **Day 2 · Tue 27 Oct · `U3·L7`** — still life and the ethics of abundance: what did this cost, who paid.
- **Day 3 · Wed 28 Oct · `U3·L8`** — individual reflection: your own table, without turning it into a memory piece. You finish the deliverable today.
- **Day 4 · Thu 29 Oct · `U3·L9`** — what a shared meal makes visible that talking does not.
- **Day 5 · Fri 30 Oct · `U3·L10`** — seminar: is hospitality generosity or obligation. LB4 is posted for whoever is present.

## Assessment

- **The your-table reflection** — written and finished Wed 2026-10-28.
- **LB4** — Fri 2026-10-30, posted for the students in the room. G12 students are not penalised for being at Seniors' Day.
- Mid-term grades are due Thu 2026-10-29 and rest on A2 plus participation.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 05: Titian to Manet

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-2-narrative-myth-and-allegory|Unit 2]] · Lesson 05 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-04-telling-it-without-words|L04]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-06-the-table-as-moral-space|L06]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U2·L9`–`U2·L16` |
| Days | 8 |
| Dates | Fri 2026-10-09 to Fri 2026-10-16 |
| Deliverable | A2: Unit 2 comparative essay |
| Counts toward | Mid-term grades, due Thu 2026-10-29 |
| Homework | Reading on Olympia's reception, 30 min, set day 4, due day 6 |

## Goal

This lesson opens with a debrief and closes with feedback, with the essay in between. Your returned storyboards come back first, so you see your own decisions before looking at anyone else's. The essay is a comparison, not a list: a list is not a comparison. Make one claim about two pictures, and count differences only where they carry meaning.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Thu 8 Oct · `U2·L9`** — break-homework debrief: storyboards returned, ten minutes silent with the criteria, find the frame where the sequence stopped being legible. CS3 posted.
- **Day 2 · Fri 9 Oct · `U2·L10`** — Gentileschi's Judith, close looking: describe the violence in the painting before you describe the painter.
- **Day 3 · Sat 10 Oct · `U2·L11`** — Judith and the problem of the witness: who does the picture put you next to? Make-up Saturday, Sports Day after lunch.
- **Day 4 · Mon 12 Oct · `U2·L12`** — Titian, *Venus of Urbino*: what does it ask you to accept as natural. Reception reading set.
- **Day 5 · Tue 13 Oct · `U2·L13`** — Manet, *Olympia*, silent, no context: locate the exact refusals Manet is making of Titian, one by one.
- **Day 6 · Wed 14 Oct · `U2·L14`** — comparison workshop: build the claim, test it against the pictures.
- **Day 7 · Thu 15 Oct · `U2·L15`** — **A2 sits.** In class, closed, 40 minutes.
- **Day 8 · Fri 16 Oct · `U2·L16`** — first read-back and the bridge into food.

## Assessment

- **CS3** — Fri 2026-10-09, the storyboard debrief posting.
- **A2** — in-class comparative essay, closed, 40 minutes: one claim about two pictures, evidenced from the pictures rather than the reception. Announced Wed 2026-09-30. The sitting date will be confirmed in class.
- A2 counts toward the mid-term grades (due Thu 2026-10-29).

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 09: Food, the Table and Ethics

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics|Unit 3]] · Lesson 09 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-08-heda-and-the-image-of-work|L08]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-10-berger-and-the-reproduction|L10]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U3·L18`–`U3·L22` |
| Days | 5 |
| Dates | Mon 2026-11-16 to Fri 2026-11-20 |
| Deliverable | A3: Unit 3 essay on food, the table and ethics |
| Homework | None — you write the essay in class |

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## What the lesson covers

Over five days you look at three artists, then write the essay, and close with a session that leads into Unit 4.

- **Day 1 · Mon 16 Nov · `U3·L18`** — Cassatt: one domestic interior read as a workplace, treating domestic labour as labour.
- **Day 2 · Tue 17 Nov · `U3·L19`** — Rosler, *Semiotics of the Kitchen*: a six-minute video, shown twice.
- **Day 3 · Wed 18 Nov · `U3·L20`** — Weems, *Kitchen Table Series*, viewed in sequence: what a table does when it is the same table every time.
- **Day 4 · Thu 19 Nov · `U3·L21`** — **A3.** In-class essay, 40 minutes: one claim about food and ethics, argued from the pictures themselves.
- **Day 5 · Fri 20 Nov · `U3·L22`** — hyperreality and the food image: what a food image is for when nobody intends to eat. This session hands off into Unit 4.

## Assessment

**A3** is an in-class essay on food, the table and ethics. It is scheduled during Day 4 (Thu 2026-11-17); the exact sitting day will be confirmed in class.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 08: Heda and the Image of Work

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics|Unit 3]] · Lesson 08 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-07-the-split-weeks|L07]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-09-food-the-table-and-ethics|L09]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U3·L11`–`U3·L17` |
| Days | 7 teaching days across 10 calendar days |
| Dates | Mon 2026-11-02 to Fri 2026-11-13 (G11 out Wed 11-04 to Fri 11-06) |
| Template | STUDY |
| Deliverable | CS4 Heda paragraph, plus LB5 |
| Feeds | A3 |
| Homework | Reading on Dutch still life and the inventory, 30 min, set day 4, due day 6 |

This block runs as a short Monday–Tuesday start, then a break while G11 Juniors are out (Wed 11-04 to Fri 11-06), then a full week. A3 is announced on Tuesday 3 November, the last session both halves of the class attend before the deadline, so attendance that day matters.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 2 Nov · `U3·L11`** — Heda: the still life as inventory. Name every object and who owns it.
- **Day 2 · Tue 3 Nov · `U3·L12`** — **A3 announced** (Unit 3 essay, due Thu 19 Nov) in the first ten minutes. Then Heda again, the same painting, taken further than yesterday. LB5 posted. Then three days out.
- **Day 3 · Mon 9 Nov · `U3·L13`** — Leonardo, *The Last Supper*: find the moment Leonardo chose, and say why that one and not the one before it.
- **Day 4 · Tue 10 Nov · `U3·L14`** — Bruegel and Steen: the disorderly table, taken seriously as a subject rather than comedy.
- **Day 5 · Wed 11 Nov · `U3·L15`** — the Qingming scroll, unrolled, followed by food along its length — 35 of the 45 minutes.
- **Day 6 · Thu 12 Nov · `U3·L16`** — the moral image of work: when did work become worth painting, and for whom.
- **Day 7 · Fri 13 Nov · `U3·L17`** — write the Heda paragraph; swap and cut one adjective from someone else's. CS4 collected. Direct practice for A3.

## Assessment

- **LB5** — Tue 2026-11-03, posted from the second Heda session.
- **CS4** — Fri 2026-11-13, the Heda paragraph: one paragraph that earns every adjective in it.

These dates may shift when the school publishes its calendar for this period.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 10: Berger and the Reproduction

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity|Unit 4]] · Lesson 10 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-09-food-the-table-and-ethics|L09]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-11-aura-and-the-poor-image|L11]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U4·L1`–`U4·L7` |
| Days | 7, across two split weeks |
| Dates | Mon 2026-11-23 to Tue 2026-12-01 (G12 out Thu–Fri 11-26/27; G11 out from 12-02) |
| Template | STUDY |
| Deliverable | The caption exercise, plus LB6 and LB7 |
| Feeds | A4 |
| Homework | *Ways of Seeing*, episode one or the first essay, 30 min, set day 1, due day 3 |

These dates are provisional and may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Goal

Seven teaching days across two weeks, with the class split in both. The core content is in days one to three; later days are self-contained. A4 is announced on day seven, Tuesday 1 December, which is the last session both halves attend. Nothing is assessed in this lesson beyond two postings.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 23 Nov · `U4·L1`** — Berger's argument stated flat: a reproduction is not a neutral copy. The first objection, taken seriously. Reading set.
- **Day 2 · Tue 24 Nov · `U4·L2`** — one painting, four contexts: change the words around an image and watch its meaning move.
- **Day 3 · Wed 25 Nov · `U4·L3`** — seminar: what did the painting lose, what did it gain, by becoming reproducible. LB6 posted.
- **Day 4 · Thu 26 Nov · `U4·L4`** — self-contained (G12 out): a reproduction read as an object with decisions of its own.
- **Day 5 · Fri 27 Nov · `U4·L5`** — self-contained (G12 out): write the caption that changes the picture, then write the honest one. The deliverable.
- **Day 6 · Mon 30 Nov · `U4·L6`** — Godard, *Goodbye to Language*, one extract: sit with an image that refuses to add up, and stay with it. Standalone.
- **Day 7 · Tue 1 Dec · `U4·L7`** — A4 announced (Unit 4 synthesis, due Thu 17 Dec), then the floating world as a market, not a mood. LB7 posted.

## Assessment

- **LB6** — Wed 2026-11-25, seminar posting.
- **LB7** — Tue 2026-12-01, floating-world posting.
- Tue 2026-12-01 is the last session both cohorts attend before the deadline.
- The caption exercise is the ungraded deliverable both halves complete.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 13: The Artworld and the Brief

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity|Unit 4]] · Lesson 13 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-12-photography-and-after|L12]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-14-the-retrospective|L14]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Days | 3 |
| Dates | Mon 2026-12-21 to Wed 2026-12-23 |
| Deliverable | Winter break homework: the retrospective brief, plus LB8 |
| Homework | Break homework: three works that changed how you see, with reasons; 3–4 hours across the break, set on day 3 |

These dates are provisional and may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## What this lesson covers

Over three days you reach the end of the story the course has told since the caves: what makes something art when looking cannot tell you. Duchamp and Warhol arrive here. Day three sets the break homework, which is also the announcement of the retrospective. This is the last session before the break, so the notice covers the whole holiday.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 21 Dec** — Danto: the artworld and the end of art.
- **Day 2 · Tue 22 Dec** — Warhol, Duchamp, and pluralism: following the object out of the studio and into the institution — who decided, and where. LB8 posted. End-of-Semester Activity today: expect a short session.
- **Day 3 · Wed 23 Dec** — Retrospective announced (Thu 7 Jan, in class), then the break homework: three works, with reasons, 3–4 hours, not more.

## Assessment

- **LB8** — posted Tue 2026-12-22.
- **Retrospective announced** Wed 2026-12-23 — 15 days' notice covering the holiday; the brief doubles as the break homework.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 11: Aura and the Poor Image

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity|Unit 4]] · Lesson 11 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-10-berger-and-the-reproduction|L10]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-12-photography-and-after|L12]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U4·L8`–`U4·L11` |
| Days | 4 |
| Dates | Mon 2026-12-07 to Thu 2026-12-10 |
| Template | MAKE |
| Deliverable | CS5 poor-image lab |
| Feeds | A4 |
| Homework | Benjamin, the aura passages only, 30 min, set day 1, due day 2 |

## Goal

You start with ukiyo-e — a print is already many — so that Benjamin reads as a description of something you have seen rather than as abstract theory. You then move to Steyerl, the writer you will draw on most directly. The lab on day four is your practice for A4, and the one session all term where you are asked to make something worse on purpose.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Mon 7 Dec · `U4·L8`** — ukiyo-e: a print is many, not one; the market that made them. Benjamin set for homework.
- **Day 2 · Tue 8 Dec · `U4·L9`** — aura, defined precisely enough to argue with Benjamin about it — then to argue with it.
- **Day 3 · Wed 9 Dec · `U4·L10`** — Steyerl, the poor image: defend the low-resolution image as a thing with its own value; what circulation gives.
- **Day 4 · Thu 10 Dec · `U4·L11`** — the poor-image lab: degrade, compress, re-photograph, screenshot the screenshot; then fifteen minutes writing exactly what changed. CS5 collected.

## Assessment

- **CS5** — poor-image lab write-up, collected on day 4 (Thu 2026-12-10): describe exactly what changed, not just that it got worse.

These dates fall in December 2026 and may shift when the school publishes its calendar for that period; the exact CS5 collection day will be confirmed in class.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 12: Photography and After

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity|Unit 4]] · Lesson 12 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-11-aura-and-the-poor-image|L11]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-13-the-artworld-and-the-brief|L13]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Session codes | `U4·L12`–`U4·L17` |
| Days | 6 |
| Dates | Fri 2026-12-11 to Fri 2026-12-18 |
| Deliverable | A4: Unit 4 synthesis |
| Homework | Sontag, first essay, 30 min, set day 2, due day 3 |

These dates may shift once the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Goal

A4 is the last thing you submit this semester. It clears the roughly 1 January late-work cutoff by two weeks, which means nothing in this stretch can be handed in late. The synthesis is the second constellation of the year; the Unit 1 boards go back up on day four so you can measure this one against your first.

## How it runs

- **Day 1 · Fri 11 Dec · `U4·L12`** — what the poor image gains: what circulation gives that quality takes.
- **Day 2 · Mon 14 Dec · `U4·L13`** — photography and the claim to truth: why we believe photographs, and whether we should. Sontag reading set.
- **Day 3 · Tue 15 Dec · `U4·L14`** — Sontag: photographing as an act on the world.
- **Day 4 · Wed 16 Dec · `U4·L15`** — avant-garde and kitsch: test Greenberg's line and find the place it stops holding. Unit 1 boards back on the wall.
- **Day 5 · Thu 17 Dec · `U4·L16`** — **A4 sits**: build the constellation and write the argument, 40 minutes.
- **Day 6 · Fri 18 Dec · `U4·L17`** — synthesis debrief: work returned with the criteria, and the one move that would have sharpened it.

## Assessment

- **A4** — Unit 4 synthesis constellation, 40 minutes. Marked on arguing across the whole unit, not a selection of favorites. The exact sitting date will be confirmed in class.
- Review is in-lesson on day six — the only in-lesson review this term, done while the work is still live.

# Art Appreciation S1 Lesson 14: The Retrospective

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-5-semester-close-and-retrospective|Unit 5]] · Lesson 14 of 15 · ← [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-13-the-artworld-and-the-brief|L13]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-15-return|L15]] →

## Goal

The whole semester, read back as one argument. The retrospective is the End of Term assessment (20%), sat in class rather than in the exam window. This is the third constellation of the year. Everything you have made is available; the Unit 1 boards and the Unit 4 syntheses go up on the walls before Monday.

# Art Appreciation S1 Unit 1: Origins and Ancestral Memory

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 1 of 5 · next: [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-2-narrative-myth-and-allegory|Unit 2 →]]

## What this unit does

This unit sets up the course's method: Warburg's atlas — thematic and non-linear, grouping images by resemblance rather than date. You start from the earliest images (Chauvet, Lascaux), move through Neolithic settlement and ritual objects (asking what an object does, not just what it depicts), and end by building your first board — the course in miniature. The same move returns at larger scale in A4 and the End-of-Term retrospective.

The darkroom session on day three shows how an image is something light did to a surface, not just a picture on a screen. This idea carries into Unit 4 in December.

Key theorist: Aby Warburg. Key material: cave imagery, Neolithic figures, ritual objects.

## Dates and span

- **Weeks 1–3**: Tue 2026-09-01 to Fri 2026-09-18. 14 sessions (3 + 6 + 5), no holidays.

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-01-opening-the-atlas|Lesson 01: Opening the Atlas]] — 3 days; the atlas method, looking before explaining, then the darkroom; CS1.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-02-what-survives-and-why|Lesson 02: What Survives, and Why]] — 6 days; Lascaux and Chauvet, argued from the wall. A panel reading may be added; this will be confirmed in class. LB1.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-03-the-first-board|Lesson 03: The First Board]] — 5 days; Neolithic and ritual objects, constellation workshop, then the A1 board build and justification.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What |
|---|---|---|
| CS1 | Fri 2026-09-04 | Darkroom reflection, written in the room |
| LB1 | Fri 2026-09-11 | Seminar posting: take a position and move it |
| **A1 sits** | **Fri 2026-09-18** | Unit 1 board and justification — every adjacency justified |

Full detail in the [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|full register]].

# Art Appreciation S1 Unit 5: Semester Close and Retrospective

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 5 of 5 · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity|← Unit 4]]

## What this unit does

You read the whole semester back as one argument. The End-of-Term retrospective is the third constellation of the year — after the A1 board and the A4 synthesis, the same move at its largest scale. You inventory first (everything on the wall, no arranging), then defend the three works from your break homework against someone who chose differently, do a last workshop, sit the exam, and take a forward look. After the exam window (which holds no exam for this course — it is the marking window), there are three uncoded, weightless return days: one-to-one feedback, Semester 2 goals about a practice rather than a grade, and a first look ahead to Semester 2.

## Dates and span

- **Weeks 18–19 + return**: Mon 2027-01-04 to Fri 2027-01-08, then Tue 2027-01-19. 8 sessions (5 + 3).
- You sit the retrospective **Tue 2027-01-05, in class** — outside the exam window.
- Fri 2027-01-08 is the last teaching day of the semester.
- Exam window Mon 2027-01-11 to Mon 2027-01-18: no exam for this course; used to mark the retrospective.
- **Grades and comments due Wed 2027-01-20.** LB9 (Tue 2027-01-19) falls after this deadline and carries no Semester 1 weight — it counts toward Semester 2.
- Last school day Fri 2027-01-22. Winter vacation 2027-01-24 to 02-21.

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-14-the-retrospective|Lesson 14: The Retrospective]] — 5 days; inventory wall, three-works debrief, constellation workshop, EoT sits Tue 2027-01-05, forward look.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-15-return|Lesson 15: Return]] — 3 uncoded days; work returned one-to-one (≈8 minutes per student), Semester 2 goals; LB9.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What |
|---|---|---|
| Retrospective announced | Wed 2026-12-23 | In lesson 13; 15 days' notice; brief doubles as break homework (three works that changed how you see, 3–4 h) |
| **EoT retrospective sits** | **Tue 2027-01-05** | Semester-close constellation, in class — End of Term category, 20% |
| LB9 | Tue 2027-01-19 | Posted after the grades deadline — no S1 weight, counts toward S2 |

Full detail in the [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|full register]].

# Art Appreciation S1 Unit 4: Images, Mediation and Modernity

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 4 of 5 · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics|← Unit 3]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-5-semester-close-and-retrospective|Unit 5 →]]

## What this unit does

This unit works through the course's main theorists, in teaching order: Berger (a reproduction is not a neutral copy), Benjamin (aura, introduced through ukiyo-e — "a print is already many"), Steyerl (the poor image, defended and then made in the lab), Sontag (photographing as an act on the world), Greenberg (avant-garde and kitsch), and Danto (the artworld, with Warhol and Duchamp). Your A4 is the second synthesis constellation of the year, building on the work you began in lesson 01. The unit's last lesson sets the retrospective brief as break homework, which belongs to Unit 5's End of Term.

## Dates and span

- **Weeks 13–17**: Mon 2026-11-23 to Wed 2026-12-23. 20 sessions (7 + 4 + 6 + 3).
- **Week 13: G12 out Thu 2026-11-26 and Fri 2026-11-27** (IB Training + Seniors); content is front-loaded into days 1–3 of lesson 10.
- Tue 2026-12-01 falls before the UC application deadline; G12 carries a reduced load, and nothing beyond two postings is assessed in lesson 10.
- **G11 Juniors Days Wed 2026-12-02 to Fri 2026-12-04: no class.**
- Foreign Culture Day Fri 2026-12-11 falls on lesson 12 day 1; nothing is graded.
- End-of-Semester Activity Tue 2026-12-22; only LB8 is due.
- Christmas break begins Thu 2026-12-24. The late-work cutoff is around 2027-01-01, so A4 is the last thing submitted this semester. These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for that period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-10-berger-and-the-reproduction|Lesson 10: Berger and the Reproduction]] — 7 days across two split weeks; four contexts, caption exercise, Godard on day 7; LB6, LB7.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-11-aura-and-the-poor-image|Lesson 11: Aura and the Poor Image]] — 4 days; ukiyo-e, Benjamin, Steyerl, degradation lab; CS5.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-12-photography-and-after|Lesson 12: Photography and After]] — 6 days; claim to truth, Sontag, Greenberg; A4 sits day 5, review in-lesson day 6.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-13-the-artworld-and-the-brief|Lesson 13: The Artworld and the Brief]] — 3 days; Danto, Warhol/Duchamp/pluralism; retrospective announced + break homework; LB8.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What |
|---|---|---|
| LB6 | Wed 2026-11-25 | Seminar posting: what the painting lost and gained by reproduction |
| LB7 | Tue 2026-12-01 | Posting from the floating-world session |
| CS5 | Wed 2026-12-09 | Poor-image lab: degrade an image on purpose, describe exactly what changed |
| **A4 sits** | **Tue 2026-12-15** | Unit 4 synthesis constellation — argues across the whole unit |
| LB8 | Tue 2026-12-22 | Posting on the End-of-Semester Activity day |
| Retrospective announced | Wed 2026-12-23 | Brief doubles as break homework |

Full detail in the [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|full register]].

# Art Appreciation S1 Unit 2: Narrative, Myth and Allegory

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 2 of 5 · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-1-origins-and-ancestral-memory|← Unit 1]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics|Unit 3 →]]

## What this unit does

This unit is about how images carry a story without words. The first half builds your vocabulary — narrative (where a still image places time), myth (shared shorthand that needs an audience who already knows the story), allegory (the image that means something elsewhere), and cycle — and turns it into a made thing: a ten-frame storyboard that has to work on framing alone.

The second half centres on a pair of paintings: Titian's *Venus of Urbino* (what it asks you to accept as natural) against Manet's *Olympia* (where its refusals are located), with Gentileschi's *Judith Slaying Holofernes* as a close-looking study of the problem of the witness. The critique flow (describe, account, judge) is taught from scratch at the top of Lesson 04. The storyboard is direct rehearsal for the A2 comparison — both are about reading what a picture decided.

## Dates and span

- **Weeks 4–7**: Sun 2026-09-20 to Fri 2026-10-16. 16 sessions (8 + 8) across a broken calendar.
- Opens on the **Sunday make-up day**, 2026-09-20.
- Mid-Autumn Festival: Fri 2026-09-25 / Sun 2026-09-27. **National Day holiday: 2026-10-01 to 10-07.**
- **Saturday make-up day** 2026-10-10, with Sports Day in the afternoon (short session).
- A2 sits Tue 2026-10-13.
- Foreign Culture Day: Fri 2026-10-16.

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Lessons

- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-04-telling-it-without-words|Lesson 04: Telling It Without Words]] — 8 days, the longest lesson in the semester: critique flow, narrative/myth/allegory/cycle, storyboard begun on day 8, then homework over the break; CS2, LB2.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-05-titian-to-manet|Lesson 05: Titian to Manet]] — 8 days: storyboard debrief, Gentileschi (twice), Titian, Manet, comparison workshop, A2 on day 7; CS3.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What |
|---|---|---|
| LB2 | Wed 2026-09-23 | Posting from the cycle-reading session |
| CS2 | Tue 2026-09-29 | Ten-frame storyboard, collected in class |
| CS3 | Fri 2026-10-09 | Storyboard debrief — find the frame where the sequence stopped being legible |
| **A2 sits** | **Tue 2026-10-13** | In-class comparative essay, Titian vs Manet, closed |

A2 carries the mid-term grades (due Thu 2026-10-29), so it is marked within a week rather than the usual five days. Full detail is in the [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|full register]].

# Art Appreciation S1 Unit 3: Food, the Table and Ethics

[[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]] · Unit 3 of 5 · [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-2-narrative-myth-and-allegory|← Unit 2]] | [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity|Unit 4 →]]

## What this unit does

This unit treats the table as a place where rules get enforced, not just a setting. It opens on film — Ang Lee's *Eat Drink Man Woman* — then works through still life as inventory (Heda, the same painting two days running), Leonardo's *Last Supper* as a chosen moment, the disorder of Bruegel and Steen, and the Qingming scroll walked along its printed length. It closes on three artists the earlier paintings could not anticipate — Cassatt (domestic labour as labour), Rosler's *Semiotics of the Kitchen*, and Weems's *Kitchen Table Series* — and the A3 essay. This is the longest unit of the semester.

## Dates and span

Dates below may shift if the school publishes a revised calendar for this period.

- **Weeks 8–12**: Mon 2026-10-19 to Fri 2026-11-20. **18 teaching days / 23 periods**, 4 days a week, with a double on Tuesdays. Week 10 loses its Wednesday and Friday to the G11 Juniors Days.
- **Screening: Tue 2026-10-20 (double, 90') + Wed 2026-10-21 (45') = 135 minutes.**
- Autumn Field Trip is either Fri 2026-10-23 or Sat 2026-10-24; the exact day will be confirmed in class. Both readings apply.
- **Week 9 (2026-10-26 to 10-30): G12 are out all week at Seniors' Day.** These are four self-contained sessions — nothing carries over and nothing is assessable.
- Mid-term grades are due Thu 2026-10-29, based on A2 plus participation.
- **G11 Juniors Days Wed 2026-11-04 to Fri 2026-11-06: no class.** Lesson 08 runs over 7 teaching days across 10 calendar days.
- Foreign Culture Day Fri 2026-11-20 — lessons run. The essay is already in by then (A3 sits Tue 11-17).

## Lessons

- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-06-the-table-as-moral-space|Lesson 06: The Table as Moral Space]] — 5 days; the frame, the screening, one meal read as an argument; LB3.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-07-the-split-weeks|Lesson 07: The Split Weeks]] — 5 self-contained days while G12 is away; the your-table reflection; LB4.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-08-heda-and-the-image-of-work|Lesson 08: Heda and the Image of Work]] — 7 days; Heda twice, Last Supper, Bruegel/Steen, Qingming, the moral image of work on day 2; CS4, LB5.
- [[classes/art-appreciation/lesson-plans/art-appreciation-s1-lesson-09-food-the-table-and-ethics|Lesson 09: Food, the Table and Ethics]] — 5 days; Cassatt, Rosler, Weems, A3 on day 4, hyperreality coda.

## Student handouts (available 2026-07-19)

The following handouts are the materials for these activities — vocabulary, plain-language overviews, key questions, and prompts.

| This unit | Handout |
|---|---|
| L06 screening | Lesson 1 — *Eat Drink Man Woman*, incl. three viewing-note questions |
| L08 Heda | Lesson 3 — Heda and Still Life |
| L08 shared meal | Lesson 4 — The Shared Meal · Lesson 4a — The Moral Image of Work |
| L09 Rosler | Lesson 4b — Martha Rosler Intro · Lesson 5 — Semiotics of the Kitchen |
| L09 hyperreality coda | Lesson 6 — Hyperreality + Paratheatre |

> [!note] The screening
> *Eat Drink Man Woman* runs 123 minutes and is viewed in class or split across sessions. Three questions carry across the split: how food works as communication; what moral meanings attach to cooking; and how eating together both unites and divides.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What |
|---|---|---|
| LB3 | Fri 2026-10-23 | Seminar posting: food, class, who is not in the picture |
| LB4 | Fri 2026-10-30 | Seminar posting, for whoever is present — G12 not penalised |
| LB5 | Tue 2026-11-03 | Posting from the second Heda session |
| CS4 | Fri 2026-11-13 | The Heda paragraph — every adjective earned; direct practice for A3 |
| **A3 sits** | **Tue 2026-11-17** | In-class essay: food, the table and ethics, argued from the pictures |

Full detail is in the [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|full register]].

> [[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · Semester 2 · previous: [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-1-plan|Semester 1 Plan]]

Spring 2027 (roughly Feb 22 – Jun), mixed G11 and G12. This semester moves from *reading images* to *the structures of the art world* — patronage, markets, and visibility — and closes on the **Atlas Book capstone**, which is also your Semester 2 End-of-Term piece. There is no sit-down mock exam for this course.

The teaching-week slots below (T4, T7, etc.) will be matched to calendar dates and confirmed in class.

| Catalog Entry | T9 |
| Career submission packet | T12 |

## Semester notes

- There is no external paper and no mock exam. The whole semester builds toward the **Atlas Book**, your capstone and End-of-Term piece.
- **Qingming week** (Qingming falls Mon 2027-04-05, but the holiday date floats): a free Atlas-Book production block.
- **May Day** (roughly 05-01 to 05-05): revision and Atlas-Book production only.

These spring dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for that period.

# 9607 S1 Lesson 01: The Four Key Concepts

[[classes/media-studies/media-studies|Media Studies]] · [[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-1-introduction|Unit 1]] · Lesson 01 of 16 · [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-02-semiotics-how-images-mean|L02]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Days | 3 — Tue single, Wed double, Fri single |
| Dates | Tue 2026-09-01 · Wed 09-02 · Fri 09-04 |
| Band/Unit | U1 Introduction ([[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-1-introduction\|S1 Unit 1]]) |
| AO focus | AO2 |
| Deliverable | CS1 analytical paragraph — denotation only |
| Feeds | A1 |
| Homework | Blog set-up; first EN/ZH lexicon entries (≥5 by Friday) |

These dates may shift when the school publishes the calendar for this period.

## Goal

By the end of week 1 you will use Language, Representation, Industry and Audience as working tools, and show it by writing a pure-denotation paragraph — describing what is there while holding back all interpretation. Describing before meaning is the foundation A1 is marked on.

## How it runs

- **D1 (Tue, single):** introductions, getting to know the group, why Media Studies, and how the course runs. Closes with the two-year map. Both EN and ZH are welcome in the room — the lexicon starts this week.
- **D2 (Wed, double):** the four Key Concepts introduced through one shared text, with pairs applying each concept orally; then the mediation demo — the same event cropped, framed and captioned three ways; a Key Concepts card sort in threes (24 EN/ZH cards), with every placement justified aloud; review looks at the sorts that disagreed and why.
- **D3 (Fri, single):** a denotation-only paragraph modeled live; you write CS1 in 25 minutes with sentence frames available; exit swap checks for smuggled connotation.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| CS1 | Fri 2026-09-04 | Analytical paragraph, denotation only, written in class | AO2 |

CS1 is announced on day 1, so you have at least 72 hours' notice before you write it on Friday. See the [[classes/media-studies/assessments/9607-s1-assessments|full register]].

classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-.body:
# 9607 S1 Lesson 02: Semiotics — How Images Mean

[[classes/media-studies/media-studies|Media Studies]] · [[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-2-media-language|Unit 2]] · Lesson 02 of 16 · ← [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-01-the-four-key-concepts|L01]] | [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-03-sign-types-and-codes|L03]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Days | 4 — Mon+Tue two-day block, Wed double, Fri single |
| Dates | Mon 2026-09-07 · Tue 09-08 · Wed 09-09 · Fri 09-11 |
| Band/Unit | U2 Media Language ([[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-2-media-language\|S1 Unit 2]]) |
| AO focus | AO1 |
| Deliverable | CS2 annotated media text from daily life |
| Feeds | A1 |
| Homework | CS2 annotation task, capped at 30 min per day |

## Goal

You take a sign apart and put it back together. Monday and Tuesday run as one idea: what a sign shows versus what it suggests, using Saussure's terms. Wednesday looks at address — who the text thinks you are — and closes with a decision that matters later: the media area you will keep working with through to May. On Friday your CS2 annotations are due and get tested through anchorage work.

## How it runs

- **D1–2 (Mon+Tue block):** Saussure input (signifier, signified, sign); station work separating "shows" from "suggests" on eight images. Day 2 opens with a five-minute retrieval, then you build and challenge connotation chains in pairs, using EN/ZH examples to show how connotation varies across cultures.
- **D3 (Wed, double):** modes of address across three contrasting texts; you profile who each text thinks it is talking to. In the final 15 minutes you commit to your media area and log it on the blog with one line of justification.
- **D4 (Fri, single):** anchorage and relay through caption-swap exercises (same image, three captions, meaning moves); you submit your CS2 annotations and peer-check them against the exemplar before the teacher collects them.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| CS2 | Fri 2026-09-11 | Annotated everyday media text, ≥6 accurate code labels; homework-based, set day 1 (72-hour rule) | AO1 |

Milestone inside: you commit to your media area on **Wed 09-09** — this feeds the C2 case-study document opened in [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-12-institutions-and-section-a-craft|L12]]. See the [[classes/media-studies/assessments/9607-s1-assessments|full register]].

# Art Appreciation Semester 1 Plan

> [!note] Your weekly schedule
> **Mon P4 (45') · Tue P6+7 (double, 90') · Wed P1 (45') · Fri P2 (45').** Four teaching days, five periods, one double, 225 minutes a week. **There is no Thursday session.**

> [[classes/art-appreciation/art-appreciation|Art Appreciation]] · Semester 1 · next: [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-semester-2-outline|S2 outline]]

**Mixed G11+G12, Autumn 2026-27.** 15 deliverable-based lessons across **65 teaching days / 82 periods**. A lesson is one deliverable, not one day: if a day is lost, the deliverable does not move — the beats compress and the cut beat is named. Dates follow the [[calendar|school calendar]].

Because this is a **mixed G11+G12 room, both cohorts' absence blocks affect the course** — G11 Juniors Days (11-04→06, 12-02→04) and G12 Seniors' Days (the 10-26 week, 11-26/27). Attendance matters: a lost Wednesday costs a whole unit stage.

## The five units

| Unit | Weeks | Gate |
|---|---|---|
| [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-1-origins-and-ancestral-memory\|U1 Origins and Ancestral Memory]] | W1–3 | **A1** board, Fri 2026-09-18 |
| [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-2-narrative-myth-and-allegory\|U2 Narrative, Myth and Allegory]] | W4–7 | **A2** Titian-vs-Manet essay, **Tue 2026-10-13** |
| [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-3-food-the-table-and-ethics\|U3 Food, the Table and Ethics]] | W8–12 | **A3** essay, **Tue 2026-11-17** |
| [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-4-images-mediation-and-modernity\|U4 Images, Mediation and Modernity]] | W13–17 | **A4** synthesis, **Tue 2026-12-15** |
| [[classes/art-appreciation/unit-plans/art-appreciation-s1-unit-5-semester-close-and-retrospective\|U5 Semester Close and Retrospective]] | W19 | **EoT** retrospective, **Tue 2027-01-05** |

Each unit page lists its lessons. Full register: [[classes/art-appreciation/assessments/art-appreciation-s1-assessments|S1 Assessments]].

**Theorist spine in teaching order: Warburg → Berger → Benjamin → Steyerl → Sontag → Greenberg → Danto.** The three constellations (A1 board, A4 synthesis, EoT retrospective) are the same move at increasing scale.

## Week by week

| Wk | Teaching days | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| W1 | Tue 09-01 **·** Wed 09-02 **·** Fri 09-04 | Atlas method (Tue double opens the course); Wed = A-Level Introduction Days; **CS1** darkroom reflection + Fri |
| W2 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 09-07 → 11 | Lascaux/Chauvet — "the wall is the evidence"; **LB1** seminar Fri |
| W3 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 09-14 → 18 | Neolithic; constellation workshop on the Tue double; **you sit A1 board Fri 09-18** |
| W4 | Mon 09-21 · Tue 09-22 · Wed 09-23 | Critique flow taught cold; narrative/myth/allegory; **LB2** Wed. ⚠ Fri 09-25 is Mid-Autumn (no session). A Sun 09-20 make-up day may run — the timetable will be confirmed in class |
| W5 | Mon 09-28 · Tue 09-29 · Wed 09-30 | Storyboards (**CS2** Tue) + break homework Wed 09-30 — then 12 days of holiday |
| W6 | **Fri 10-09 only** | ⚠ **One session.** National Day removes Mon–Wed and there is no Thursday. Break debrief + **CS3** fit here. A Sat 10-10 make-up may run — confirmed in class |
| W7 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 10-12 → 16 | Titian *Venus of Urbino* / Manet *Olympia*; **you sit A2 Tue 10-13 (double)**; Fri 10-16 Foreign Culture Day (lessons run) |
| W8 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 10-19 → 23 | U3 opens; ***Eat Drink Man Woman*** — **Tue double (90') + Wed (45') = 135', covers the 123-minute film**; **LB3** Fri |
| W9 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 10-26 → 30 | ⚠ **G12 out all week.** Four self-contained sessions, nothing carries, nothing assessable; **LB4** Fri |
| W10 | Mon 11-02 · Tue 11-03 · ~~Wed 11-04~~ · ~~Fri 11-06~~ | Heda ×2 + LB5 Tue 11-03 — the last both-cohort session before the deadline. ⚠ G11 out Wed–Fri (Juniors Days) |
| W11 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 11-09 → 13 | Last Supper; Bruegel/Steen; Qingming scroll walked (Tue double); **CS4** Heda paragraph Fri |
| W12 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 11-16 → 20 | Cassatt; Rosler; Weems; **you sit A3 Tue 11-17 (double)**; Fri 11-20 Foreign Culture Day (lessons run) |
| W13 | Mon 11-23 · Tue 11-24 · Wed 11-25 · ~~Fri 11-27~~ | Berger *Ways of Seeing*; **LB6** Wed. ⚠ G12 out Fri (Seniors' Day) |
| W14 | Mon 11-30 · Tue 12-01 · ~~Wed 12-02~~ · ~~Fri 12-04~~ | Godard + LB7 Tue 12-01. ⚠ G11 out Wed–Fri (Juniors Days) |
| W15 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 12-07 → 11 | Ukiyo-e; Benjamin; Steyerl; **CS5** poor-image lab Wed 12-09; Fri 12-11 Foreign Culture Day (lessons run) |
| W16 | Mon–Tue–Wed–Fri 12-14 → 18 | Photography and truth; Sontag; Greenberg; **you sit A4 Tue 12-15 (double)** |
| W17 | Mon 12-21 · Tue 12-22 · Wed 12-23 | Danto; Warhol/Duchamp; **LB8** Tue (End-of-Semester Activity, short room); **retrospective announced Wed 12-23** + break homework |
| ~~W18~~ | — | 🚫 **Winter break, 12-24 → 01-03**, school-wide |
| W19 | Mon 01-04 · Tue 01-05 · Wed 01-06 · Fri 01-08 | Mon = inventory the semester; **you sit the EoT retrospective Tue 01-05 (double)**; Fri 01-08 last teaching day |
| W20 | — | Exam window 01-11 → 18; this course sits no exam |
| W21 | 01-19 → 21 | Work returned; 1:1 feedback (~8 min each); S2 goal-setting (**LB9**, no S1 weight). Return days sit outside the normal timetable |

## Standing protocols

Session spine 45': 5 land / 10 look / 20 work / 10 close (one student says what changed, one what is stuck — not optional). Critique: **describe → account → judge, never out of order**; the describe round is written before it is spoken. Assessment review within 5 working days: criteria + 10 minutes' silence to find your own gap, one strength + one move, written; the next lesson opens with the move. Homework ≤30 min/day; break homework 3–4 h per ten-day holiday.

classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-.body:
# 9607 S1 Lesson 03: Sign Types and Codes

[[classes/media-studies/media-studies|Media Studies]] · [[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-2-media-language|Unit 2]] · Lesson 03 of 16 · ← [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-02-semiotics-how-images-mean|L02]] | [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-04-genre-and-camera|L04]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Days | 4 — Mon+Tue two-day block, Wed double, Fri single |
| Dates | Mon 2026-09-14 · Tue 09-15 · Wed 09-16 · Fri 09-18 |
| Band/Unit | U2 Media Language ([[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-2-media-language\|S1 Unit 2]]) |
| AO focus | AO1 |
| Deliverable | CS3 vocabulary quiz, 20 min |
| Feeds | A1 |
| Homework | Lexicon revision toward CS3; no new tasks |

## What you will do

You consolidate two weeks of terminology into vocabulary you can use under pressure. You learn Peirce's triad as a second way to sort signs, and the four code families that you will use to analyse every extract from here on. On Wednesday you read stills you have never seen before using vocabulary alone. Friday's quiz records your vocabulary.

## How it runs

- **D1–2 (Mon+Tue block):** Peirce input (icon, index, symbol); sorting gallery of 12 signs by how they mean; day 2 retrieval, then the four code families (technical, symbolic, written, audio) mapped onto two contrasting stills; you build your personal code checklist.
- **D3 (Wed, double):** cold-reading protocol modeled on one still; three unseen TV-drama stills, three minutes silent noticing each, then code-labeled write-up; review compares readings and rewards precision over quantity.
- **D4 (Fri, single):** you sit CS3 in the first half — 20 items, 20 minutes; self-mark immediately against definitions; correct missed terms into the lexicon on the spot; exit — teach one term back to a partner.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| CS3 | Fri 2026-09-18 | Vocabulary quiz, 20 items in 20 min; target ≥15/20 or missed terms logged with corrections | AO1 |

See the [[classes/media-studies/assessments/9607-s1-assessments|full register]].

# 9607 S1 Lesson 04: Genre and Camera

[[classes/media-studies/media-studies|Media Studies]] · [[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-2-media-language|Unit 2]] · Lesson 04 of 16 · ← [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-03-sign-types-and-codes|L03]] | [[classes/media-studies/lesson-plans/9607-s1-lesson-05-narrative-and-the-planning-lock|L05]] →

## At a glance

| | |
|---|---|
| Days | 3 — Mon single, Tue single, Wed double (no Friday class: Mid-Autumn Festival) |
| Dates | Mon 2026-09-21 · Tue 09-22 · Wed 09-23 |
| Band/Unit | U2 Media Language ([[classes/media-studies/unit-plans/9607-s1-unit-2-media-language\|S1 Unit 2]]) |
| AO focus | AO2 |
| Deliverable | CS4 genre and camera analytical paragraph — direct practice for A1 |
| Feeds | A1 |
| Homework | None this week |

## What you will learn

Genre theory and camera vocabulary come together in this lesson because A1 asks you to use them in the same paragraph. You study Altman and Neale, who treat genre not as a box of tropes but as an agreement between industry and audience — the version that earns marks. For camera work, you learn verbs before adjectives: what the camera does, then what it does to the viewer. In the Wednesday double you prove the whole lesson in one CS4 paragraph.

## Session by session

- **D1 (Mon, single):** Altman and Neale reduced to the exam-usable core — repetition and difference; you map one genre you know onto the industry–audience contract; exit question: why does the industry need genre more than we do.
- **D2 (Tue, single):** camera vocabulary (shots, angles, movement); paired rapid-fire naming drills on a shot montage; a second pass adds effect — name the move, then name what it does to you; exit ticket: three shots, three effects.
- **D3 (Wed, double):** CS4 — you watch an unlabeled clip menu, attribute genre from codes alone, and write an analytical paragraph in exam register; the final 20 minutes are peer-checked against the A1 criteria this task rehearses.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| CS4 | Wed 2026-09-23 | Genre and camera analytical paragraph from an unlabeled clip; direct practice for A1 | AO2 |

## What this covers

You learn representation theory and then apply it directly to your own work. The week opens with A2 going in and ends with two standalone sessions where your rough cut is screened as an audience would see it and audited for stereotypes you may not have noticed — while there is still production time to fix them.

## Session by session

- **D1 (Mon, single):** Submit your A2 blog URLs in the first five minutes. Then: representation as construction — the same person represented three ways, naming selection and combination.
- **D2 (Tue, single):** Hall's encoding/decoding — dominant, negotiated, oppositional; place your own honest reading of one text and defend the placement.
- **D3 (Wed, double):** stereotyping as power — who benefits, who pays; case work on two contrasting stereotype examples; reading positions revisited on both.
- **D4 (Fri, single):** absence and selective construction — what is not in the frame; inventory one text for its absences and what each absence achieves.
- **D5 (Mon 11-02, standalone):** rough-cut screening under audience conditions — no introductions, no apologies; complete structured response cards while watching.
- **D6 (Tue 11-03, standalone):** stereotype audit of your own product using the week's full toolkit (construction, positions, power, absence); **LB3 posted**; draft your fix list.

| Feeds | A4, End of Term Exam |

## What you will learn

Gaze theory as open questions rather than fixed answers. Over the two-day block you build van Zoonen and Mulvey; the Wednesday double tests Mulvey against what audiences actually watch across decades; and bell hooks asks who a text never expected to be watching. CS6 closes the week: one close-reading paragraph with a theorist working inside it — the same move A4 and the exam reward.

## How it runs

- **D1–2 (Mon+Tue block):** van Zoonen — gender as performance, worked on two ads; day 2 retrieval, then Mulvey stated precisely: the look of the camera, the look of characters, the look of the audience.
- **D3 (Wed, double):** paired clips across decades screened and compared — does the gaze hold, shift, or invert? Groups argue a verdict with timecoded evidence, and the review collects the best evidence lines.
- **D4 (Fri, single):** bell hooks — the oppositional gaze as refusal and rereading; **you write CS6** today (one close-reading paragraph, theorist embedded). Portfolio is due Fri 11-27, exactly 14 days later.

## Assessment

| Item | Date | What | AO |
|---|---|---|---|
| CS6 | Fri 2026-11-13 | Representation close-reading paragraph where the theorist does analytical work rather than decoration | AO2 |
